concept: **文字挂机传奇:王者战神**: This is a text-based idle RPG where the player's character automatically engages in combat against enemies in a continuous loop, with battle outcomes and resource gains displayed in a real-time text log. Players interact by tapping on on-screen notifications to collect accumulated gold, experience points, and crafting materials, which accrue even when the game is closed. Progression involves spending these resources in menu-driven interfaces to upgrade core character stats like Attack, Defense, and Health Points, or to unlock and enhance passive and active skills. New equipment, categorized by rarity (e.g., 1-star to 5-star), is automatically equipped if superior or can be manually managed, providing significant stat boosts. The game advances through sequential zones, each featuring increasingly difficult enemies and boss encounters that require a minimum power level to overcome, with no permanent loss state as the character simply respawns to continue idling.
name: 文字挂机传奇:王者战神
developer: Shaanxi Yangfuman Network Technology Co., Ltd.

concept: **Zombie Night Terror**: Zombie Night Terror is a real-time strategy puzzle game where players indirectly control a zombie horde to infect humans and overcome environmental obstacles across 2D pixel-art levels. Players issue commands by tapping on individual zombies or dragging to select multiple, then tapping ability icons from a UI bar to activate specific mutations like "Overlord" to guide movement, "Exploder" to destroy weak walls, or "Runner" for increased speed. Each mutation costs a specific amount of "DNA points," a resource gained by converting humans or completing level objectives, and has a cooldown before reuse. The core loop involves strategically using mutations to navigate levels, convert all 10-20 humans per stage into new zombies to grow the horde, and destroy destructible environmental elements to create new paths. A level is won when all humans are infected or a specific objective is met, while failure occurs if the entire zombie horde is eliminated by human defenses or environmental hazards.
name: Zombie Night Terror

concept: **Worms™ 4**: Worms 4 is a turn-based artillery strategy game where two teams of worms battle on a destructible 2D landscape, taking turns to move and attack. During a turn, players control a single worm by dragging to move a limited distance across the terrain and tapping to perform a jump, then select a weapon from their arsenal. Weapons are aimed by swiping on the screen to adjust trajectory and power, with a final tap to fire, causing explosions that inflict damage (e.g., 20-50 HP) and deform the environment. Progression involves completing 80 single-player missions or winning synchronous online multiplayer matches, which award coins and experience used to unlock and upgrade over 100 unique weapons and utilities, enhancing their damage, blast radius, or utility duration. Players can customize their worms with cosmetic items purchased with coins, and the game concludes when one team eliminates all opposing worms by reducing their 100 HP to zero.
name: Worms™ 4

concept: **Broken Sword: Reforged**: Broken Sword: Reforged is a classic point-and-click adventure game where players guide protagonist George Stobbart through a linear narrative by interacting with static 2D environments. Players tap on interactive hotspots to examine objects, collect inventory items, initiate dialogue with NPCs, or move George to new areas within a scene. The inventory is accessed via a dedicated icon, allowing players to drag and drop collected items onto environmental elements or combine them with other inventory items to solve a variety of logic-based puzzles. Progression is driven by successfully solving these puzzles, which often require multiple steps and careful observation, unlocking new locations and advancing the overarching mystery. The game features no direct failure states, allowing unlimited attempts at puzzles, and players can unlock achievements for specific story milestones or creative puzzle solutions.
name: Broken Sword: Reforged

concept: **Arcadia Sports:** Arcadia Sports functions as a central hub presenting a collection of 12 distinct sports-themed arcade mini-games, accessible from a main menu interface where players tap to select a title. Each mini-game, such as "Hoop Fever" (tap to shoot) or "Formula Race" (swipe to steer), offers unique, simplified touch controls and specific win conditions, typically focusing on achieving high scores or completing objectives within a single session. Progression is primarily score-based, with players aiming to set new personal bests and compete on Game Center leaderboards for each individual game, though no explicit unlock system for new games is detailed beyond future content updates. Difficulty within each mini-game scales dynamically, for example, by increasing opponent speeds or introducing more complex obstacle patterns over time, challenging players to maintain performance for longer durations. The core loop involves selecting a game, engaging in a quick, score-driven session, and then returning to the main menu to try another game or improve a previous score, with progress saved via iCloud.
name: Arcadia Sports - スポーツ系アーケードゲーム

concept: **Poly Bridge 2:** Poly Bridge 2 is a physics-based puzzle game where players construct bridges to guide vehicles across gaps, using a drag-and-drop interface to place structural elements like roads, wood, steel, and the new Spring material between fixed anchor points. Players select materials from a palette and draw segments by tapping and dragging between nodes, forming a network of supports and roadways, with each material having specific cost and stress resistance properties. After construction, players initiate a simulation phase by tapping a "Start" button, observing how vehicles (e.g., cars, trucks) attempt to cross the bridge, with structural integrity determined by a custom physics engine that calculates stress on each segment. A level is successfully completed when all designated vehicles safely reach their destination without the bridge collapsing, adhering to a strict budget limit (e.g., $10,000) and a maximum number of structural elements. Progression involves completing over 100 pre-designed levels, each introducing new environmental challenges, vehicle types, and mechanics like hydraulic pistons or the bouncy Spring material, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex terrain and tighter budget constraints.
name: Poly Bridge 2

concept: **Evoland 2:** Evoland 2 is a story-driven adventure game that dynamically shifts its core gameplay mechanics, visual style, and control schemes to emulate various video game genres throughout its 20+ hour narrative. Players primarily navigate a world map and dungeons using a virtual joystick for movement and a context-sensitive action button for interactions, but these controls transform to suit the current genre, such as a tap-to-shoot interface during a top-down shooter segment or directional swipes for attacks in a 3D fighting sequence. Progression is linear and tied to the overarching narrative, with players unlocking new time periods and corresponding game genres (e.g., 8-bit 2D RPG, 16-bit platformer, 3D fighting game, trading card game) as they advance through story quests and solve environmental puzzles. Each genre transition introduces new enemies, combat systems, and puzzle types, requiring players to adapt their strategies, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex enemy patterns, tougher boss encounters, and more intricate puzzle designs. The core loop involves exploring diverse environments, engaging in genre-specific combat, collecting key items, and interacting with NPCs to advance the plot, ultimately leading to the resolution of the spacetime continuum disorder.
name: Evoland 2

concept: **Factory Balls:** Factory Balls is a logic puzzle game where players must replicate a target ball design, displayed on a shipping box, by applying a sequence of tools to a plain white ball using a tap-based interface. Players select tools from a fixed palette (e.g., paint buckets, pliers, various spectacle-shaped masks) by tapping them, which apply specific colors or manipulate the ball's shape, with the order of operations being crucial as subsequent actions may overwrite or modify previous ones. Each level presents a unique target pattern (e.g., orange with white eyes and black pupils), and players must deduce the correct combination and sequence of tool applications to transform the initial white ball into the exact replica. There are no time limits or penalties for incorrect moves, encouraging experimentation, and a "Reset" button allows players to restart the current ball's transformation at any point. Progression occurs across 200 distinct levels, with difficulty scaling through increasingly intricate target designs, a wider array of available tools, and more complex layering requirements, challenging players to master advanced logical sequencing to achieve the desired outcome.
name: Factory Balls

concept: **Type:Rider:** Players control two rolling dots (••) through 2D platforming levels themed around the history of typography, using left/right swipes to move and a tap anywhere on the screen to jump, with a second tap mid-air for a double jump. The core loop involves navigating intricate environments constructed from letters and symbols, solving physics-based puzzles by pushing blocks, activating switches by landing on specific character parts, or timing jumps across moving typographic elements. Progression occurs across 10 distinct worlds, each representing a different font or era, unlocking sequentially upon completion, with difficulty scaling as platforming challenges become more precise and puzzles require multi-step solutions. The goal is to reach the end of each level, with failure (falling into gaps or hitting hazards) resulting in an immediate respawn at the last checkpoint.
name: Type:Rider

concept: **Freeways:** Players act as a freeway engineer, viewing a top-down map with pre-defined traffic entry and exit points, and use a finger to draw multi-lane road segments connecting these points. The core loop involves designing an interchange, then tapping a "Play" button to simulate traffic flow for 60 seconds, observing vehicle movement and identifying congestion points. The objective is to achieve a 3-star rating by ensuring all vehicles reach their destinations efficiently without significant traffic jams (e.g., no more than 10 cars stuck for 5 consecutive seconds), with difficulty increasing through new levels that introduce more complex layouts, higher traffic volumes (e.g., 50-150 cars per minute), and varied terrain. Successfully completing a level unlocks the next, while failure to meet efficiency targets requires redesigning the road network.
name: Freeways

concept: **RGB Express:** Players manage a color-coded delivery service on a grid-based map, drawing distinct paths for multiple trucks (typically 2-4) by dragging a finger from each truck to its destination. Each truck must pick up its matching colored package and deliver it to the corresponding colored house, with the core loop involving path planning for all trucks before tapping "Play" to simulate their simultaneous movement. Progression spans 400 levels, gradually introducing new mechanics like bridges (allowing one-way or limited crossings), buttons (activating/deactivating barriers), and cargo swap zones, which increase the complexity of collision avoidance and delivery sequence. The objective is to successfully deliver all packages without any truck collisions, with failure requiring a redesign of the paths.
name: RGB Express

concept: **Doodle Devil™:** Players combine existing elements to discover new, "evil" elements in a puzzle-driven creation game, starting with four basic elements like Fire, Earth, Wind, and Air displayed in a left-hand panel. The core mechanic involves dragging two elements from the panel onto a central creation area to attempt a combination (e.g., Human + Sin = Sinner), with successful discoveries adding the new element to the player's available inventory. Progression is measured by the total number of elements discovered, with hundreds of unique combinations available across various categories like demons, monsters, and sins, and difficulty scaling as combinations become less intuitive and require more abstract thought. The primary goal is to discover all possible elements, with side modes like "Demon Battle Card Game" offering alternative gameplay loops.
name: Doodle Devil™

concept: EvoCreo 2 is a turn-based monster collection RPG where players, as a novice trainer, navigate an open world by tapping on the screen to move their character and interact with NPCs. The core loop involves encountering wild Creo, initiating turn-based battles where players select actions like "Attack," "Skill," or "Switch Creo" from a UI menu, aiming to capture them by reducing their health and using specific capture items. Captured Creo can be trained and evolved indefinitely, gaining access to over 200 skills and 100 traits, which players strategically assign to customize their team of up to 6 Creo with roles like tank or support. Progression involves completing over 50 story missions, uncovering conspiracies, and challenging increasingly difficult trainers and legendary Creo across diverse biomes, culminating in Coliseum battles to climb rankings and optimize builds for endgame content.
name: EvoCreo 2: モンスター対戦・育成RPG MMO

concept: Tsuro is a digital board game where 2 to 8 players take turns placing path tiles onto a 6x6 grid board, using tap-and-drag controls to select and orient a tile before placing it adjacent to their current stone. After placing a tile, the player's stone automatically moves along the newly formed path until it reaches an endpoint, potentially connecting to other players' paths or leading off the board. The core objective in classic mode is to be the last player remaining on the board, as any stone forced off the edge is eliminated from the game. Difficulty scales with the number of players and the strategic complexity of AI opponents (with 3 distinct personalities), while progression is tracked through over 50 achievements for "Dragon Master" and new modes like "Loop Battle" and "Longest Path" offer alternative win conditions.
name: Tsuro - 경로의 게임

concept: Deus Ex GO is a turn-based stealth puzzle game where players control agent Adam Jensen by tapping on adjacent grid spaces to move him one step at a time. After each player move, all enemies on the map also move one step according to their patrol patterns or AI logic, creating a dynamic puzzle environment. The core objective is to reach an exit point on the grid, requiring players to utilize stealth to avoid enemy line-of-sight, hack terminals to control turrets or alter level layouts, and activate augmentations like temporary invisibility for 1 turn. Progression unfolds across over 50 story puzzles, introducing new enemy types such as guards, turrets, drones, and walkers, each with distinct movement and attack patterns that increase strategic complexity. Daily challenges and an "Infinite Play" mode with procedurally generated puzzles provide continuous content, while a Puzzle Creator allows players to design and share their own levels.
name: Deus Ex GO

concept: "Du bist der Einzige hier" is a first-person horror adventure game where players navigate a surreal dreamscape, a blend of memories and nightmares, using a virtual joystick on the left side of the screen for movement and swiping on the right side to control the camera. The core gameplay involves exploring the environment, interacting with objects by tapping them, and solving environmental puzzles to uncover clues related to a central mystery. Progression is non-linear, focused on discovering hidden pathways and specific interactions that lead to one of multiple distinct endings. The game's difficulty scales through the increasing psychological tension, jump scares, and the complexity of piecing together the narrative fragments required to unlock the true escape path.
name: Du bist der Einzige hier

concept: **Ridiculous Fishing - A Tale of Redemption** Players initiate a fishing attempt by tapping to cast, then tilt their device left and right to steer the lure, avoiding all fish as it descends to reach maximum depth, which can extend to over 500 units. Once the lure hits a fish or reaches its maximum depth, it automatically begins ascending, and players must rapidly tap the screen to fire their equipped weapon, shooting as many fish as possible that appear on screen. Each caught fish converts into cash based on its rarity and size (e.g., a common fish yields 5 coins, a rare one 50), which players use in a shop UI to purchase upgrades like longer fishing lines (increasing max depth by 50 units), faster lures (increasing descent speed by 15%), and more powerful weapons (increasing projectile count by 1 or damage by 20%). Reaching specific cumulative cash milestones (e.g., 1000, 5000, 10000 coins) or catching unique fish species unlocks new fishing locations (continents), each featuring distinct fish populations, increased depth limits, and more challenging fish patterns during descent and ascent. The primary objective is to complete Billy's narrative journey by progressing through all fishing spots, catching all 60+ unique fish species, and fully upgrading all available equipment to maximize catch efficiency and income.
name: Ridiculous Fishing - A Tale of Redemption

concept: **ダンジョントレーサー (Dungeon Tracer)** Players navigate a grid-based dungeon by swiping to connect 3 or more adjacent, identical tiles (e.g., red attack, blue defense, green healing, yellow mana) to activate their effects, consuming the connected tiles and causing new ones to cascade down. Connecting attack tiles deals damage to visible enemies based on the number of tiles and player's attack stat (e.g., 5 damage per tile), while defense tiles reduce incoming damage for the next turn by a percentage (e.g., 10% per tile), and healing tiles restore a fixed amount of HP (e.g., 5 HP per tile). Defeating enemies grants experience points to level up the player character, increasing base stats like HP and attack by 5% per level, and also drops gold and items; gold is spent in a shop UI to purchase new equipment (e.g., swords, armor) or upgrade existing items up to 5 tiers, each increasing specific stats by 10-20%. The dungeon layout and enemy encounters are procedurally generated, with difficulty scaling by increasing enemy HP by 15% and attack by 10% every 5 floors, introducing new enemy types with unique abilities (e.g., poison, stun) and boss encounters every 10 floors. Players aim to survive as long as possible, with the game ending when their character's HP reaches 0, and progression is tracked via a global leaderboard based on floors cleared and total score accumulated.
name: ダンジョントレーサー

concept: **Extreme Landings Pro** Players pilot various aircraft using a virtual joystick for pitch and roll, a rudder slider for yaw, and a throttle slider for engine power, while interacting with a detailed 3D cockpit UI by tapping switches and buttons to manage systems like landing gear, flaps (with 5 distinct settings), and spoilers. The core gameplay involves managing complex aircraft systems, including individual engine ignition, fuel balancing (e.g., jettisoning excess fuel), and responding to over 5,000 potential emergency scenarios like engine fires or hydraulic failures by following specific procedural checklists via UI prompts. Navigation is achieved using instruments like the Primary Flight Display (PFD) and Navigation Display (ND, showing 8000+ waypoints), coupled with an Instrument Landing System (ILS) for precision approaches to over 500 accurate airport replicas, all influenced by real-time weather conditions including microbursts and icing. Progression occurs through completing 36 structured missions, each with specific objectives (e.g., "land safely with one engine out"), and 216 challenges that test specific piloting skills, earning pilot ranking points based on performance metrics like landing accuracy (e.g., within 10 meters of centerline) and emergency resolution speed. A mission is successfully completed upon a safe landing within specified parameters (e.g., runway alignment, vertical speed below 300 ft/min), while failure results from crashing, exceeding critical system limits (e.g., overspeeding landing gear), or failing to meet mission objectives within time or safety constraints.
name: Extreme Landings Pro

concept: **Bob Esponja: The Cosmic Shake:** Players control SpongeBob in a 3D platforming adventure, navigating 7 distinct Wish Worlds using a virtual joystick for movement on the left side of the screen and context-sensitive action buttons on the right for jumping, attacking, and interacting. Progression involves unlocking new traversal abilities, such as a "Karate Kick" to break specific barriers or a "Hook-Swing" to cross large gaps, which are granted upon completing story quests or defeating boss enemies. These abilities are crucial for accessing previously unreachable areas and solving environmental puzzles, with difficulty scaling through more intricate platforming challenges and enemies requiring specific ability counters. Players can collect a unique in-game currency to unlock over 30 cosmetic costumes, offering visual customization without affecting gameplay. The core loop involves exploring worlds, overcoming obstacles, defeating enemies, and completing objectives to advance the narrative, with failure occurring upon losing all 3 health points or falling into hazards, respawning at the last checkpoint.
name: Bob Esponja: The Cosmic Shake

concept: **Beat the Boss 2 (17+):** Players inflict damage upon a stationary boss character across 7 interactive stages, primarily by tapping the screen to activate the currently equipped weapon or swiping for specific weapon actions like dragging a blade or aiming a projectile. The core objective is to deplete the boss's health bar, which resets upon stage completion, allowing progression to the next, more resilient boss. In-game currency, earned from each successful hit and by completing "funny player objectives" (e.g., "Hit the boss 50 times with a rubber chicken"), is used to unlock over 80 unique weapons and upgrade their damage output by 10% per level. Each weapon possesses distinct damage values, attack animations, and physics-based reactions, providing varied visual and tactile feedback. Difficulty scales as bosses gain increased health and damage resistance in subsequent stages, encouraging players to unlock and upgrade their arsenal to maximize damage per second.
name: Beat the Boss 2 (17+)

concept: **던전 추적자 (Dungeon Tracer):** Players navigate a grid-based dungeon by connecting adjacent tiles of the same type (e.g., 3 sword tiles for attack, 3 shield tiles for defense, 3 potion tiles for healing), using intuitive swipe controls to draw paths. Connecting attack tiles damages nearby enemies, while defense tiles reduce incoming damage from enemy attacks that occur after the player's turn. Defeating enemies grants experience points, allowing the player character to level up and improve core stats (e.g., +5 Attack, +10 HP), and unlock new abilities from a pool of 30. Dungeons are procedurally generated, ensuring a unique layout and enemy placement in each run, with 25 types of special monsters. Players collect gold from defeated enemies and treasure chests, which can be spent between runs to purchase and upgrade over 400 unique items and apply 20 powerful upgrades to existing gear. Difficulty scales across 4 selectable modes, increasing enemy health, damage, and spawn rates (e.g., 10% faster monster spawns on Hard difficulty). The primary goal is to survive as long as possible and achieve the highest score on the global leaderboard; a run ends when the player's health points (e.g., starting at 100 HP) reach zero, losing all temporary progress but retaining permanent item unlocks and upgrades.
name: 던전 추적자

concept: **Radiation City:** Players navigate a vast, open-world Pripyat in a first-person perspective, using a virtual joystick on the left for movement and swiping the right side of the screen to control the camera and aim. The core loop involves scavenging buildings for resources (e.g., 5 units of scrap metal, 1 can of food) and crafting essential survival items like melee weapons, firearms, and medical supplies. Survival mechanics require constant management of hunger, thirst, and radiation meters, which deplete every 60 seconds and must be replenished with specific consumables found or crafted. Combat is real-time, engaging mutated creatures and hostile survivors using equipped weapons, with damage output dependent on weapon type and enemy weak points. Progression involves unlocking new crafting recipes, discovering safe camps for fast travel and storage, and repairing vehicles that require fuel to operate, while difficulty scales through increasingly irradiated zones and more numerous, tougher enemies. Failure occurs if health, hunger, thirst, or radiation meters reach zero, resulting in death and respawn at the last activated safe camp.
name: Radiation City

concept: **Ta Mère En Slip:** This is a party guessing game for 2 to 12 players, where one active player holds the iOS device to their forehead, displaying a randomly generated combination of a "character" and an "action" card (e.g., "a singer" + "lighting a fire") that only other players can see. Other players provide verbal clues to help the active player guess the combination without directly revealing the words, while the active player swipes down to pass on a difficult combination or swipes up to confirm a correct guess, advancing to the next card combination. Each round is governed by a customizable timer, which can be set for durations like 30, 60, or 90 seconds, and the objective is to correctly guess as many combinations as possible before time expires. The game features over 300 unique card combinations, with players able to add custom cards, and the winner is typically the player or team with the highest score after a predetermined number of rounds or total correct guesses.
name: Ta Mère En Slip

concept: **MiniGames Watch Games 24-in-1 (Brick Break):** Brick Break is a classic falling block puzzle game where players manipulate various tetromino-shaped blocks descending from the top of a vertical playfield. Players use a finger swipe left/right on the screen (or the Digital Crown on Apple Watch) to horizontally move the current falling block, tap the screen to rotate its orientation 90 degrees clockwise, and swipe down to instantly drop the block into place. The objective is to strategically stack blocks to form complete horizontal lines, which then clear from the playfield and award points, causing all blocks above to shift down. The game continuously increases the fall speed of blocks and the frequency of new block spawns as the player's score rises, escalating difficulty until blocks stack to the top of the playfield, ending the game. The primary goal is to achieve the highest possible score by clearing lines efficiently.
name: MiniGames Watch Games 24-in-1

concept: **マインスイーパQ プレミアム (Minesweeper Q Premium):** Minesweeper Q Premium is a classic puzzle game played on a grid of hidden squares, where players must uncover all non-mine squares without detonating any mines. In "Standard Mode," players tap a square to reveal its content; if it's a mine, the game ends, but if it's empty, it reveals a number indicating how many mines are in the 8 adjacent squares, or automatically clears a larger area if no adjacent mines exist. Players long-press a square to place a flag, marking it as a suspected mine, and can tap an already revealed number to "quick open" all surrounding unflagged squares if the number of flags matches the displayed digit. The game offers three classic difficulty levels (Beginner, Intermediate, Expert) with preset grid sizes and mine counts (e.g., 9x9 with 10 mines, 16x16 with 40 mines), alongside custom options for board dimensions and mine density. Progression is tracked via high scores for each difficulty, with the objective being to clear the board in the fastest time possible, and players can switch between "Standard" and "Quick Flag" modes by tapping an empty grid space for optimized flagging or opening.
name: マインスイーパQ プレミアム

concept: **SteamWorld Quest:** SteamWorld Quest is a turn-based role-playing game where players control a party of heroes engaging in combat using a customizable deck of "punch-cards." During combat, players are dealt a hand of cards from their collective party deck and strategically select 3-5 cards per turn to execute actions like attacking, defending, or casting spells against enemy units. Each hero contributes unique cards to the overall deck, and players can craft and upgrade their deck from a pool of over 100 distinct punch-cards, allowing for diverse combat strategies. Progression involves earning experience points (XP) from battles to level up heroes, increasing their stats and unlocking new card options, alongside collecting gold to purchase additional cards or equipment. The game features a linear adventure across various hand-drawn worlds, with the objective being to defeat all enemies in a given encounter to advance, while a loss occurs if all party members are incapacitated.
name: SteamWorld Quest

concept: **20 Minutes Till Dawn:** 20 Minutes Till Dawn is a top-down, rogue-lite survival shooter where players control a chosen hero who automatically fires their equipped weapon at incoming hordes of monsters. Players navigate the character using a virtual joystick or by dragging their finger across the screen, dodging enemy projectiles and melee attacks across a single arena map. After defeating a certain number of enemies or surviving timed waves, players are presented with a choice of 2-3 random upgrades from a pool of over 50 unique options, which can enhance weapon damage, fire rate, character health, or unlock new abilities, forming unique "builds" for each 10-20 minute run. The primary objective is to survive for the full 20-minute duration against continuously spawning and increasingly aggressive enemy types, with the game ending immediately if the player's health points drop to zero. Meta-progression involves unlocking new characters and weapons between runs, each offering distinct starting stats and upgrade synergies.
name: 20 Minutes Till Dawn

concept: **Alien Shooter - Survive:** Players control a soldier in a top-down, fixed-screen arena, moving with a virtual joystick on the left and firing their equipped weapon with a virtual button on the right, with an optional auto-aim system. Hordes of alien monsters continuously spawn from the screen edges, increasing in density and speed every 20 seconds, and the player must eliminate them before their health points (starting at 100 HP) are depleted. Upon defeating monsters, they drop experience points and occasionally in-game currency, which are automatically collected when the player moves over them. Collecting enough experience points levels up the player, unlocking new active abilities like a 5-second temporary shield or passive upgrades such as a 10% damage increase, while currency can be spent between runs to unlock new weapons with distinct fire rates and damage outputs (e.g., a shotgun dealing 50 damage per shot). The game's objective is to survive for the longest duration or clear the maximum number of monster waves, with the run ending immediately if the player's HP reaches zero.
name: Alien Shooter - Survive

concept: **귀곡팔황 (Guigubahuang):** This open-world Xianxia RPG features a player character who cultivates spiritual power to grow stronger, navigating a vast map by tapping to move or using a virtual joystick, and interacting with NPCs and objects via context-sensitive taps. Progression involves advancing through distinct cultivation stages (e.g., Qi Condensation, Foundation Establishment), each requiring specific resource collection and successful completion of mini-game challenges to break through. Combat is real-time, where players tap ability icons on a hotbar to unleash skills against various "요수" (demonic beasts) and rival cultivators, with damage output determined by learned "공법" (cultivation techniques) and equipped gear. Players can manage a sect, completing missions as a disciple, assigning tasks as an elder, or defending resource nodes as a sect leader, while also crafting "연단" (potions) and "부적" (talismans) from gathered materials. The game features three unique "삼대신기" (Divine Artifacts) chosen at the start, offering powerful abilities like reviving allies or taming bosses, with the ultimate goal being to achieve immortality, unify the "팔황" (Eight Desolations), or defeat powerful "신선" (immortals).
name: 귀곡팔황

concept: **FLOTTENMANÖVER (Battleship):** FLOTTENMANÖVER offers two distinct modes of naval combat on a grid-based battlefield, controlled by tapping grid squares to select targets and confirming with a "Fire" button. In Classic Mode, two players secretly place 5 ships of varying lengths (e.g., a 5-square Carrier, a 4-square Battleship) on a 10x10 grid, then take turns calling out coordinates, with the first player to sink all opponent's ships winning. Commander Mode introduces unique naval commanders, each possessing 3 distinct special abilities (e.g., "Salvo" to fire 3 shots in a line, "Sonar Ping" to reveal ships in a 3x3 area) that consume 1-3 resource points per use, with 1 resource point generated each turn. This mode may feature larger grids (e.g., 12x12) and new L-shaped or T-shaped ship configurations, requiring players to strategically deploy abilities to gain an advantage. Progression involves completing daily missions like "Sink 5 ships with Commander X" to earn medals, increase player rank, and unlock new commanders or cosmetic ship skins.
name: FLOTTENMANÖVER

concept: **Bus Simulator:** Players control officially licensed buses using a virtual steering wheel or tilt controls for steering, and virtual pedals for acceleration and braking, navigating a detailed open-world city named Havensburg. The core gameplay involves driving along predefined routes, adhering to traffic laws and schedules, stopping precisely at designated bus stops to pick up and drop off passengers. Successfully completing routes and campaign missions, which might include objectives like "Complete Route 101 in under 15 minutes with zero accidents," earns the player credits and experience points. Credits are used to purchase new bus models from 10 licensed manufacturers (e.g., Mercedes-Benz, Volvo), unlock additional city districts (e.g., port, old town), and expand the public transport network by activating new routes. Progression is tied to completing missions to unlock content and accumulating credits to build a thriving bus company, with the ultimate goal being to acquire all bus types and establish the most extensive transport network in Havensburg.
name: Bus Simulator

concept: **斯特恩大陆 - 巨龙的威胁 (Stern Continent - Threat of the Dragon):** This management simulation game tasks players with developing a small animal village by tapping on buildings and resources to interact and manage production. Players assign villagers to gather raw materials like wood and stone, which are then processed in specialized buildings (e.g., a sawmill for wood, a forge for metal) into refined goods. New hunter types are unlocked through village progression, allowing players to recruit and train them, equipping them with crafted weapons and armor made from processed materials. Hunters are then formed into an "Alliance" (e.g., 3-member party) and dispatched on expeditions to combat "巨型生物" (giant creatures) in semi-automated battles, with success yielding rare resources and experience. Players can also craft "药剂" (potions) to aid hunters and engage with "贸易商船" (trade ships) arriving every 12 hours to exchange goods. The core loop involves resource management, crafting, and hunter deployment, with the ultimate objective being to strengthen the village and its hunters sufficiently to defeat the menacing giant dragon.
name: 斯特恩大陆 - 巨龙的威胁

concept: **WORMS** Worms is a turn-based tactical game where players control a team of 4 worms on a destructible 2D battlefield. During a player's turn, they have 30 seconds to move using on-screen left/right arrows, jump by swiping up, and select a weapon from a UI menu. Weapons like the Bazooka (dealing 25-50 HP damage) or Holy Hand Grenade (75 HP damage) are aimed by dragging a finger across the screen to adjust trajectory and power, then released to fire, with wind direction displayed on the UI affecting projectiles. The objective is to eliminate all opposing worms, each starting with 100 HP, by reducing their health to zero or knocking them into environmental hazards like water. Completing 50 single-player challenges unlocks new weapons, customization items, and environments, while a "Body Count" mode offers an endless survival challenge against AI with increasing difficulty.
name: WORMS

concept: **Ready Steady Bang** Ready Steady Bang is a quick-draw reaction game where players must tap the screen as fast as possible after an audible "Bang!" cue. The game displays "Ready..." then "Steady..." before the "Bang!" sound, and tapping prematurely results in an immediate "Foul!" and loss for that round. In single-player, players face 10 progressively challenging AI gunmen, each with faster reaction times, aiming to defeat all of them. Two players can duel on a single device, each tapping their respective half of the screen, with the fastest legitimate tap winning the round. Progression involves accumulating 31 "cowboy kills" to unlock achievements, and difficulty scales by the AI's decreasing reaction time and increased likelihood of attempting a foul.
name: Ready Steady Bang

concept: **ShellShock Live** ShellShock Live is a turn-based online multiplayer tank combat game set in destructible 2D environments. During a player's turn, they use on-screen sliders or drag gestures to precisely adjust their tank's turret angle (0-180 degrees) and shot power (0-100%) before firing one of hundreds of available weapons. Each weapon possesses unique properties, such as a Bazooka dealing 50-100 HP damage or a Digger creating a large crater, with wind direction and strength displayed on the UI influencing projectile trajectories. Players earn XP from matches to level up, unlocking new tanks with varying HP (e.g., 500-1000 HP), more powerful weapons, and cosmetic gear. The objective is to eliminate all opposing tanks by reducing their HP to zero, with difficulty scaling as players unlock more complex weapons and face higher-skilled opponents.
name: ShellShock Live

concept: **盛世天下～女帝への道～ I** 盛世天下～女帝への道～ I is an interactive live-action drama where players influence the narrative by making choices at critical junctures. Gameplay involves watching 4K video segments, which periodically pause to present 2-4 on-screen dialogue or action options that players select by tapping. These choices directly impact the protagonist's "Charm," "Wisdom," and "Courage" stats, and determine the story's branching path, character relationships, and survival. The game features 16 chapters, with an "extremely high death rate" where incorrect choices lead to immediate "Game Over" states, requiring a restart from a checkpoint. Progression involves successfully navigating these choices to unlock subsequent chapters and advance the protagonist's journey to power, while collecting "exquisite items" and discovering "hidden stories" contribute to an achievement system.
name: 盛世天下～女帝への道～ I

concept: **ソニック・ザ・ヘッジホッグ 2** Sonic The Hedgehog 2 is a fast-paced 2D side-scrolling platformer where players control Sonic using an on-screen virtual D-pad for horizontal movement and a jump button to leap, with a second tap activating a spin dash. Levels are filled with loops, springs, and enemies, which are defeated by jumping on them or using a spin attack, while collecting 100 rings grants an extra life and rings protect from single hits. Progression involves completing multiple zones, each with 2-3 acts culminating in a boss battle against Dr. Eggman, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex level layouts and enemy patterns. Collecting 50 rings and entering a giant ring grants access to a 3D half-pipe Special Stage to collect 7 Chaos Emeralds, which unlock the Super Sonic transformation. Players lose a life if they take damage without rings or fall into a pit, and the game ends upon losing all lives or defeating the final boss.
name: ソニック・ザ・ヘッジホッグ 2

concept: Small World – das Brettspiel is a turn-based digital board game where 2-5 players compete to conquer territories on a hex-grid map and accumulate the most victory points over a fixed number of rounds, typically 8-10. Each turn, players select a combination of one of 14 fantasy races and one of 20 special abilities, then deploy their limited number of race tokens by tapping and dragging them onto adjacent territories to initiate conquests. A conquest requires a specific number of tokens, usually 2 for an empty territory or 1 plus existing enemy tokens for an occupied one, with a final dice roll potentially deciding contested battles. Players can choose to put a race "into decline" to score points for a few more turns while selecting a new active race, managing their empire's expansion and decline to maximize points. Progression involves unlocking additional race and special ability combinations through game achievements, while difficulty scales with the number of AI opponents and their strategic aggression levels.
name: Small World – das Brettspiel

concept: Reckless Racing 3 is an isometric arcade racing game where players compete across 36 tracks in 6 environments using one of 28 customizable cars and trucks. Players control their vehicle using customizable on-screen virtual buttons for acceleration, braking, and left/right steering, or by connecting a compatible gamepad. The core gameplay involves navigating muddy tracks, mastering drifts by initiating turns at high speed, and managing vehicle physics to outmaneuver AI opponents across various modes. In Career mode, players progress through 9 cups by winning 60 events, unlocking new vehicles and tracks, while Gymkhana mode challenges players to perform stunts and drifts for points within a time limit. Difficulty scales through increasingly aggressive AI drivers, more complex track layouts, and the need to upgrade vehicle performance (speed, acceleration, grip) using in-game currency earned from race victories.
name: Reckless Racing 3

concept: StickWars is a fast-paced castle defense game where players defend their kingdom against waves of stick figure enemies advancing from the right side of the screen towards their castle on the left. Players eliminate enemies by tapping individual stick figures to launch basic projectiles, or by flicking the screen to deploy a volley of arrows from the castle. Special abilities include shaking the device to deploy a bomber plane that drops explosives, requiring a second shake to detonate, and using multi-touch gestures to command wizards to cast powerful area-of-effect spells like meteors. Defeated stick figures drop resources, which are automatically collected and used to upgrade castle defenses or unlock new defensive structures, allowing players to survive an unlimited number of increasingly difficult rounds where enemy spawn rates and health points gradually increase. The game ends when the castle's health points, typically starting at 100, are depleted by invading enemies.
name: StickWars

concept: R-TYPE is a classic horizontal side-scrolling shoot 'em up where players control a spaceship, navigating through 8 distinct levels filled with waves of enemies and culminating in a unique boss battle. Players move their ship by dragging a finger across the screen in "Enhanced Touch mode" or by using a virtual D-pad on the left side of the screen, while tapping on the right side fires the main weapon and holding it charges a powerful beam shot. The core mechanic involves managing the detachable "Force" pod, which can be attached to the ship for frontal or rear defense and offense, or launched independently to clear paths and absorb enemy fire. Collecting power-ups dropped by specific enemies upgrades the ship's main weapon, adds secondary weapons, and enhances the Force pod's capabilities, with difficulty scaling across two settings and within levels by increasing enemy projectile density and movement speed. The player has a limited number of lives, typically 3, and the game ends upon losing all lives, with the objective being to complete all 8 challenging levels.
name: R-TYPE

concept: Sven's SudokuPad is a digital Sudoku puzzle game featuring a standard 9x9 grid, where the objective is to fill every cell with a digit from 1 to 9 such that each row, column, and 3x3 subgrid contains each digit exactly once. Players interact by tapping a specific cell to select it, then tapping a digit from a virtual numerical keypad (1-9) to enter a solution or a "pencil mark" candidate, with a toggle button switching between solution and candidate input modes. The game includes a collection of pre-designed classic Sudokus with varying difficulty levels (e.g., Easy, Medium, Hard, Expert) determined by the number of initial given digits and the complexity of required logical steps. Additionally, players can input custom Sudoku puzzles via a dedicated interface, allowing them to solve external puzzles within the app. Progression is achieved by successfully completing puzzles, with difficulty scaling based on the puzzle's inherent design rather than dynamic game changes.
name: Sven's SudokuPad

concept: Aventureros al Tren® is a turn-based digital board game where 2-5 players compete to build railway routes across a map. Each turn, players choose one action: draw two train cards (from 5 face-up or a hidden deck), draw three new destination tickets (keeping at least one), or claim a route by discarding a specific number of matching colored train cards (e.g., 3 red cards for a 3-segment red route). Players tap on visible cards or decks to draw, and tap/drag on the map to select and confirm route claims. The game ends when any player has 3 or fewer train pieces remaining, triggering one final round for all players. Points are awarded for claimed routes (e.g., 1 point for 1 segment, 15 points for 6 segments), completed destination tickets, and the longest continuous route, with the highest score winning.
name: Aventureros al Tren®

concept: RPG ヴィラン転生 is a pixel-art JRPG where players control a party of villainous characters, navigating a 2D overworld by tapping to move. Combat is turn-based, initiated by encountering enemies, where players tap commands like Attack, Skill, or Item from a menu for each character. The core battle mechanic involves exploiting enemy weaknesses (e.g., elemental, physical) to fill a "Break" gauge; once full, the enemy is stunned, allowing a powerful "Break Attack" for bonus damage. Players earn "Infamy" by performing villainous acts, which functions as experience points to level up characters, unlock skill points for a customizable skill tree, and enable "Class Upgrades." Dungeon exploration yields building materials to expand an evil base with facilities like a Blacksmith and Research Lab, providing gear and skill enhancements, as players progress through a fixed story to its conclusion.
name: [Premium] RPG ヴィラン転生

concept: sugar, sugar is a physics-based puzzle game where players draw lines on the screen with a single finger to guide a continuous stream of white sugar particles into designated cups. Sugar trickles from a source at the top, affected by realistic gravity, and drawn lines act as physical barriers to redirect its flow. Each of the 125 levels requires a specific amount of sugar to be collected in all cups, displayed on the UI. Progression introduces new mechanics like color filters (requiring sugar to change color before entering a matching cup), gravity switches, and teleports, increasing puzzle complexity. A level is won by filling all cups to their required capacity, and lost if the sugar source depletes before all cups are filled.
name: sugar, sugar

concept: Full Throttle Remastered is a point-and-click graphic adventure game where players control protagonist Ben Throttle to solve environmental and inventory-based puzzles. Players navigate 2D scenes by tapping on interactive hotspots, which reveal a context-sensitive action menu (e.g., Look, Talk, Use) from which an action is selected. Inventory items, accessed via a dedicated UI, are dragged and dropped onto objects or other inventory items to combine or use them, advancing the linear narrative. Dialogue trees allow players to select responses to NPCs, influencing information gathered or triggering events, while occasional motorcycle combat sequences are controlled via directional swipes to attack or dodge. Progression is purely story-driven, with success defined by solving puzzles to unlock new areas and plot points, leading to the game's conclusion.
name: Full Throttle Remastered

concept: どうぶつしょうぎ is a turn-based digital board game played on a 3x4 grid, where two players control a set of four unique animal pieces. Players tap a piece to select it, then tap a highlighted valid square to move it, aiming to capture the opponent's Lion or move their own Lion into the opponent's back row (Tri). Pieces include the Lion (moves one square in any direction), Giraffe (one square horizontally/vertically), Elephant (one square diagonally), and Chick (one square forward), which promotes to a Chicken (moves one square in any direction except backward diagonally) upon reaching the opponent's back row. Captured pieces can be "dropped" onto any empty square as the current player's piece, adding a strategic layer. A player wins by checkmating the opponent's Lion or by successfully moving their Lion into the Tri zone without immediate threat.
name: どうぶつしょうぎ(公式)

concept: **ノベルズローグ ～異世界古書堂と封印の魔女～**: Players embark on roguelike dungeon runs, entering one of four distinct "books" that each feature a unique turn-based card combat system. Within dungeons, players tap cards from their hand to execute actions like attacking enemies, defending, or applying status effects, with mana costs displayed on each card. After defeating enemy encounters, players choose one of three presented cards or relics to add to their deck, dynamically building their strategy for that specific run. Progression involves clearing all floors of a book, with difficulty scaling through increasing enemy health (e.g., +15% per difficulty level) and more complex attack patterns, and players can unlock new starting cards or permanent passive bonuses at the hub by spending resources earned from runs. A run concludes upon the player character's health reaching zero, or by defeating the final boss of the current book, which unlocks the next narrative chapter or higher difficulty tiers.
name: ノベルズローグ ～異世界古書堂と封印の魔女～

concept: **SPACEPLAN**: Players begin by tapping a central "Generate Watt" button, earning 1 Watt per tap, which is the primary currency. These Watts are spent to purchase and upgrade various potato-based devices, such as "Potato Generators," which automatically produce Watts at a set rate (e.g., 5 Watts/second) even when the game is idle. Progression involves unlocking new devices and launching narrative-advancing "probes" by spending increasingly large sums of Watts, with each device upgrade typically boosting its output by 10-20%. The game scales difficulty by requiring exponentially more Watts for subsequent unlocks and probe launches across five distinct planets and two realities, encouraging optimization of idle generation. The core loop is to generate Watts, purchase upgrades, launch probes to advance the story, and ultimately uncover all narrative secrets without any traditional loss conditions.
name: SPACEPLAN

concept: **The Bard's Tale**: Players control the Bard, navigating a vast 3D world using a virtual joystick on the left and camera swipes on the right. Real-time combat involves tapping an attack button on the right and strategically summoning up to four unique companions by playing magical songs, which consume mana and have a brief channel time. Defeating enemies and completing quests awards experience points (XP) for leveling up, granting skill points to invest in a talent tree for stat boosts like +5% damage or +10 HP. Progression is driven by character level, loot acquisition (150+ unique items with varying stats), and unlocking new companions through story advancement, with difficulty scaling via stronger enemies and bosses with higher health pools (e.g., 200 HP to 1000 HP). The game concludes upon defeating the final boss of the main questline, while player death results in respawning at the last save point.
name: The Bard's Tale

concept: **What Remains of Edith Finch**: Players navigate the colossal Finch house from a first-person perspective, swiping to look around and tapping to move forward, exploring rooms and uncovering hidden passages. Interacting with highlighted objects, such as diaries or letters, by tapping them, triggers unique narrative vignettes that transport the player into the final moments of a different Finch family member. Each vignette features distinct, context-sensitive mini-games or control schemes, like tilting the device to control a swing or swiping to operate a camera, all designed to convey the character's story. Progression is entirely narrative-driven, with the game concluding once Edith has uncovered all family stories and reached the end of her personal journey. There are no traditional win/loss conditions, combat, or puzzles, focusing solely on interactive storytelling and emotional immersion.
name: What Remains of Edith Finch

concept: **1/8192**: Players are presented with two distinct gates, one blue and one green, on each stage, and must tap one to make a choice. Upon tapping, the game immediately determines if the chosen gate is a "hit" or a "miss" based on a fixed 1/2 probability. If it's a "hit," the player advances to the next sequential stage, with the current stage number displayed on the UI. Conversely, if it's a "miss," the player's progress is instantly reset, returning them to Stage 1. The core objective is to achieve 13 consecutive "hits" to reach the final "Nirvana" stage, representing an overall 1/8192 probability, with no permanent progression or unlock systems between attempts. The game concludes upon reaching "Nirvana," or continues indefinitely until this rare sequence of successful choices is made.

Tabú - juego de fiesta oficial: Tabú is a turn-based party game where two teams compete to guess words described by a clue-giver. On their turn, a clue-giver sees a main word and a list of 4-5 "taboo" words they cannot use, while their team attempts to guess the main word within a 60-second timer. Players tap a "Correct" button for successful guesses, earning 1 point, or a "Skip" button to pass on a difficult card, typically limited to 3 skips per turn. If the clue-giver accidentally uses a taboo word, the opposing team taps a "Buzzer" button on their device, deducting 1 point from the clue-giver's team. The game progresses through a customizable number of rounds (e.g., 3-5 rounds), with the team accumulating the highest total score declared the winner. New themed word decks can be unlocked or purchased, expanding the game's vocabulary pool.
name: Tabú - juego de fiesta oficial

concept: 世界の覇者1945: This turn-based grand strategy game challenges players to conquer the world by selecting one of 11 nations across 243+ zones in 3 historical eras (1940, 1942, 1944). Players tap to select military units—Infantry (high production, normal attack), Tanks (high attack, can move again after winning combat), and Artillery (attacks without counter-attack)—then tap adjacent zones to move and initiate combat, aiming to capture cities for tax resources and ultimately enemy capitals. Units gain experience from combat, earning medals to become "aces" with enhanced combat power, while players can also deploy 17 different military cards, such as "Bunker Card" for 3 turns of defense or "Air Raid Card" on adjacent enemy forces. Progression involves completing 10 specific historical campaigns and conquering the world in conquest mode, with difficulty scaling through 5 levels and AI adapting to player actions. Special national cards, unique to 7 specific nations, can be used once every 7 turns for powerful strategic advantages.
name: 世界の覇者1945

concept: 써니캠프 스토리: Sunny Camp Story is a simulation game where players manage and expand a forest campsite by tapping and dragging to place various facilities like tent sites and campfires on a grid-based map. Guests arrive at the campsite to engage in activities such as bird watching or fishing, with their satisfaction increasing their "fan" status and potentially turning them into regulars. Players can also construct and manage a shop in a nearby town, selling camping tools; successful sales generate a "fever effect" and encourage customers to visit the player's campsite, increasing overall guest traffic. Progression involves attracting a diverse range of guests, including beginners and even animals like rabbits, and unlocking new activities and shop locations to further expand the camp's appeal and revenue. The core loop involves building, attracting guests, managing their needs, and expanding the business to achieve the highest possible campsite rating and popularity.
name: 써니캠프 스토리

concept: Death Coming: Death Coming is an isometric puzzle game where players assume the role of the Reaper, tasked with causing deaths by manipulating environmental "death traps" without directly controlling humans. From a top-down perspective, players observe the routines and idiosyncrasies of multiple targets within a level, tapping on interactive objects like loose signs or faulty machinery to trigger chain reactions that lead to their demise. The game features non-linear progression, allowing players to eliminate targets in any order, but requires precise timing as victims' AI reacts to events, changing their behavior if they witness a death or perceive danger. Minions of Light actively attempt to save targets, adding a layer of challenge, and the objective is to successfully harvest a predetermined number of souls per level to advance to new, increasingly complex scenarios.
name: Death Coming

concept: 为了吾王-多人联机策略跑团: For The King is a turn-based strategy roguelike RPG, supporting 1-3 players, where adventurers explore a procedurally generated world map by tapping to move their party, with movement points determined by dice rolls. Combat unfolds in a separate turn-based sequence, requiring strategic positioning and optimal equipment allocation for each of the "tens of classes" to defeat enemies and acquire loot. Players manage their party's inventory and skills through a menu interface, making tactical decisions on whether to split up or stay together, while facing dynamic challenges from adjustable "Chaos," "Day/Night," and "Economic Inflation" system frequencies. Each run features unique quests and events, and upon completion or party wipe, new content is unlocked, ensuring varied experiences and continuous progression. The ultimate goal is to solve the king's murder and prevent impending doom, with success heavily reliant on both strategic planning and luck.
name: 为了吾王-多人联机策略跑团
developer: Xiamen Kuaibaoyou Network Technology Co., Ltd

concept: 存在/しないあなた、と私 This psycho-horror visual novel immerses players in a surreal narrative where they guide the story by making choices presented on-screen. The core mechanic involves tapping on one of over 100 dialogue or action options, with each decision influencing the unfolding events and the player's relationship with a mysterious girl. Progression is achieved by exploring multiple narrative paths, which delve into philosophical themes of existence and reality, leading to various distinct conclusions. The game's objective is to witness a specific ending for the player and the girl, with no explicit win or loss state, but rather different narrative outcomes determined by the cumulative impact of player decisions.
name: 存在/しないあなた、と私

concept: 逆転裁判123 成歩堂セレクション This adventure visual novel is divided into two core gameplay phases: Detective Part and Courtroom Part. In the Detective Part, players tap on various locations and objects to gather "information" and "evidence" relevant to a murder case. The Courtroom Part involves listening to testimonies from "prosecutors" and "witnesses," then tapping to present collected "evidence" to expose contradictions in their statements, with a long-press option to fast-forward dialogue. Progression is achieved by successfully navigating 14 episodes across three distinct games, where each case requires players to prove their client's innocence by uncovering the truth. The game concludes a case successfully when all contradictions are resolved and the true culprit is identified, while failure occurs if too many incorrect pieces of evidence are presented, leading to a guilty verdict.
name: 逆転裁判123 成歩堂セレクション

concept: Chess Pro Chess Pro is a digital implementation of the classic board game, where players control one side of an 8x8 grid, moving 16 pieces according to standard chess rules. Players interact by tapping a piece to select it, then tapping a valid destination square to execute a move, with the game engine checking for legality. Progression is offered through 100 graded difficulty levels against an AI opponent, a training mode with a built-in coach providing hints and monitoring progress, and a rated mode that tracks player skill with an ELO appraisal system. The game features a vast opening book with over 235,000 variations, and players win by checkmating the opponent's king, while losing if their own king is checkmated.
name: Chess Pro
developer: The Chess Solitaire Sudoku Factory Ltd

concept: EvoCreo2 Catch & Tame Monsters EvoCreo 2 is an open-world monster-catching RPG where players explore the continent of Shoru by tapping to move their character and interact with NPCs or objects. The core gameplay loop involves encountering and capturing over 300 unique Creo monsters, then engaging in turn-based battles where players tap to select from 200+ learned moves for their Creo. Progression is driven by completing over 50 missions, evolving Creo, and customizing them with 100+ unique traits, with no level cap for monsters. Players win battles by strategically defeating all opponent Creo, while losing if all their own Creo are knocked out, ultimately aiming to become the Evoking Master Trainer by challenging the strongest trainers and conquering the Coliseum.
name: EvoCreo2 Catch & Tame Monsters

concept: HIARCS Chess HIARCS Chess is a sophisticated chess game where players engage in standard chess matches on an 8x8 board against a highly-rated AI opponent. Players execute moves by tapping a piece to select it, then tapping a valid destination square, with the game engine enforcing all chess rules including en passant and castling. The game features an adaptive difficulty system that continuously adjusts the AI's playing strength to match the player's evolving skill, tracked by an Elo rating updated after each game, and utilizes a large opening book with over 235,000 variations. An integrated coach provides advanced assistance, offering move options, hints, safety/quality highlights, and warnings about mistakes, while progression involves improving one's Elo rating across various handicap levels. Players win by checkmating the opponent's king, and lose if their own king is checkmated, with additional modes for position setup and game analysis using a move slider and VCR buttons.
name: HIARCS Chess

concept: **マックス・ペイン モバイル**: Max Payne Mobile is a third-person action shooter where players control Max Payne through linear, objective-based levels using a virtual joystick on the left side of the screen for movement and virtual buttons on the right for actions like shooting, reloading, and interacting. The core combat mechanic involves engaging enemies with various firearms, enhanced by "Bullet Time," activated by tapping an on-screen button, which slows time for approximately 5 seconds, consuming a regenerating Bullet Time meter. Players manage a health bar (e.g., 100 HP) and weapon-specific ammunition, collecting health packs and ammo pickups scattered throughout the environment. Progression is chapter-based, advancing the narrative by completing objectives such as clearing areas of 5-10 enemies or reaching specific checkpoints, with new weapons acquired by finding them within levels. Failure occurs if Max's health reaches zero, requiring a restart from the last checkpoint, while customizable controls allow players to adjust button layouts and aiming sensitivities.
name: マックス・ペイン モバイル

concept: **Long Drive Simulator**: Long Drive Simulator is a survival road trip game where players first prepare their car through a series of mini-games, involving sequential taps and drags to brush, rub, paint, refuel (e.g., 50 units), add engine oil, and top up water coolant. The core gameplay involves driving through a procedurally generated desert, managing three critical resources—fuel, engine oil, and water coolant—which deplete at varying rates (e.g., 1 unit per 50 miles) displayed on the UI. Players must periodically stop at randomly generated fuel stations or abandoned buildings, interacting by tapping to search for and collect replacement resources or a gun with limited bullets (e.g., 10 rounds) to defend against randomly spawning zombie rabbits. Combat involves tapping to aim and shoot at enemies, with failure occurring if any car resource reaches zero or if the player's health is depleted by creatures, while the ultimate goal is to successfully complete the long drive to the mother's house.
name: Long Drive Simulator

concept: **Kreuz & Quer – spannende Wortsuche**: Kreuz & Quer is a timed word puzzle game where players are presented with a grid of letters (e.g., 5x5) and must form valid German words by dragging their finger across adjacent letters in any direction (horizontal, vertical, diagonal, forward, backward), lifting their finger to submit the word. Points are awarded based on word length, with a bonus for words 5 letters or longer, and individual letter scores are higher for "darker" colored letters. Players compete against a customizable timer (2, 3, or 5 minutes), and can rearrange the entire letter grid by shaking their device if stuck, or activate a "Joker" from the options menu to act as a wild card. The game features endless procedurally generated boards, and progression is driven by achieving higher scores, with Game Center integration allowing for direct competition against other players on identical game boards and time limits.
name: Kreuz & Quer – spannende Wortsuche

concept: **Lapse 2: Before Zero**: Lapse 2: Before Zero is a narrative-driven adventure game where players assume the role of Pharaoh in ancient Egypt (1750 BC), making critical decisions by swiping a central event card left or right to choose between two presented options. Each choice impacts a set of four to five hidden or visible kingdom resource meters, such as population, military strength, economy, and stability, which are displayed on the UI and must be maintained within a balanced range (e.g., 20-80 out of 100). The game progresses through a continuous stream of these event cards, with the narrative unfolding based on the cumulative effects of player decisions and the state of the kingdom's resources. Failure occurs when any single resource meter falls below a critical threshold (e.g., 10) or rises above an upper limit (e.g., 90), leading to a specific game-over scenario like a rebellion or famine, while the objective is to survive as long as possible and uncover the overarching mystery.
name: Lapse 2: Before Zero

concept: **Looking for Aliens**: Looking for Aliens is a hidden-object game where players explore over 25 intricately hand-drawn scenes from an alien perspective, with each level presenting a unique environment and a list of 5-10 specific objects to locate. Players interact with the scene by tapping to zoom, pan, and tap on objects to collect them, with some items requiring sequential taps or simple environmental interactions (e.g., tapping a door to open it before finding an object inside). A hint system is available, typically with a cooldown of 30 seconds, to assist players in finding elusive items. Progression is linear, with players unlocking new locations upon successfully finding all required objects in the current scene, and bonus "side quests" or collectible alien artifacts are scattered throughout levels, contributing to overall game completion. The primary objective is to complete all 25+ unique locations by discovering hundreds of quirky objects, with no explicit loss condition other than needing to use hints.
name: Looking for Aliens

concept: **룸즈: 장난감 장인의 저택 (Rooms: The Toymaker's Mansion)** Players control a character within a single room, navigating it using a virtual joystick or directional taps. The core mechanic involves sliding adjacent rooms like a picture puzzle by tapping and dragging them into empty slots, creating new pathways for the character. The objective of each of the 144 levels, spread across 4 mansions, is to manipulate the room layout and utilize specific in-room objects (e.g., ladders, teleporters) to guide the character to a designated exit tile. Progression involves unlocking new mansions and 48 bonus basement levels, which introduce unique abilities like "phone use" to swap two non-adjacent rooms or "bomb placement" to destroy specific obstacles, increasing puzzle complexity. A level is won when the character reaches the exit, and failure occurs if the character becomes trapped or cannot reach the exit due to an unsolvable room configuration.
name: 룸즈: 장난감 장인의 저택

concept: **hocus.** Players navigate a red cube across a 2D representation of an impossible geometric structure, where paths appear to connect in ways that defy 3D logic. The core mechanic involves tapping on an adjacent path segment or a destination point to move the cube along the available routes, with the cube automatically traversing the chosen path until it reaches an intersection or endpoint. Each of the 120 hand-crafted levels presents a unique impossible shape, challenging players to find the correct sequence of movements to guide the cube from a starting point to a designated target square. Progression is linear through these levels, with difficulty scaling by increasing the complexity of the impossible geometry and the number of required moves, while an "Endless mode" generates procedurally designed puzzles for continuous play. A level is successfully completed when the cube occupies the target square, with no explicit loss condition other than failing to solve the puzzle.
name: hocus.

concept: **MILLIONAIRE TYCOON™** Players compete in a turn-based real estate trading board game, starting with a limited budget and aiming to build a million-dollar empire. Each turn, players tap a "Roll Dice" button to move their avatar across a board representing city streets, landing on properties they can purchase, upgrade with items, or collect rent from opponents. The core loop involves strategically acquiring properties to form monopolies on entire streets, which significantly increases rent collected from other players, alongside trading on a dynamic stock market and purchasing vehicles for movement bonuses. Progression in the challenging Campaign mode involves completing objectives like reaching a specific net worth or bankrupting AI opponents across 10 unique cities, with difficulty scaling dramatically as AI becomes more aggressive and resource-rich. Winning requires accumulating a target net worth or eliminating all opponents through bankruptcy, while losing occurs if a player runs out of money and cannot pay debts.
name: MILLIONAIRE TYCOON™

concept: **역전재판 123 나루호도 셀렉션 (Ace Attorney 123 Phoenix Wright Selection)** Players assume the role of a defense attorney, navigating through two distinct phases: the Detective Part and the Courtroom Part, across 14 linear episodes. During the Detective Part, players tap on various hotspots within crime scenes and locations to gather "information" and "evidence" by examining objects, interviewing witnesses, and presenting collected items. In the Courtroom Part, players tap to advance dialogue, listen to witness testimonies, and then tap "Press" to probe for more details or "Present" to select and present specific pieces of evidence from their "Court Record" to expose contradictions in testimonies. Progression is story-driven, with each episode presenting a new case that increases in complexity and narrative twists, culminating in a final verdict. Winning an episode requires successfully exposing all contradictions and proving the client's innocence, while presenting incorrect evidence or failing to find crucial contradictions results in a "Guilty" verdict and a game over.
name: 역전재판 123 나루호도 셀렉션

concept: **Brutal Street 2** Players engage in a side-scrolling beat 'em up, controlling one of eight distinct heroes using a virtual joystick for movement and tap/swipe gestures for attacks, dodges, and activating "dozens of active skills." The core combat loop involves moving through linear stages, defeating waves of various enemy types (e.g., "capangas," "brutamontes") that spawn from off-screen or vehicles, and collecting dropped HP potions and weapons. Progression unfolds across "180+ stages" divided into "five main blocks" and "three difficulty levels," where players earn experience and currency to unlock and upgrade "100+ passive skills" and equip different weapon types that alter their character's "two weapon configuration" and "weapon skill system." A stage is won by clearing all enemies and reaching the end, while losing occurs if the player's character's health points (HP) drop to zero.
name: Brutal Street 2

concept: **Shredder Chess:** Shredder Chess is a digital chess game where players engage in matches against an AI opponent or solve pre-designed puzzles on a standard 8x8 chessboard. Players interact by tapping a chess piece to select it, then tapping a valid destination square to execute a move, with illegal moves being prevented by the system. The AI opponent's strength is adjustable across multiple levels from beginner to master, or can automatically adapt to the player's skill, even mimicking human errors at lower difficulties. Progression involves solving 1000 built-in chess puzzles, tracking player performance, and calculating an Elo rating that updates after each game and is visualized in a diagram. A real-time coach system provides immediate feedback, warning players of potential mistakes during gameplay, while an analysis mode allows post-game review of moves and identification of errors.
name: Shredder Chess

concept: **Next Chess Move:** Next Chess Move functions as a powerful chess analysis tool rather than a playable game, allowing users to input any chess position for engine-driven move calculations. Players set up a board position by tapping and dragging pieces on an 8x8 digital chessboard or by pasting FEN/PGN strings into a text field. The application then processes this position using various integrated chess engines, including Stockfish 17.1 running locally and numerous remote engines like Lc0, to determine optimal moves and provide evaluation scores. The UI displays the engine's top move suggestions, along with their predicted outcomes and principal variations, allowing users to explore different lines. While not a game with traditional progression, an optional Pro subscription enhances the calculation strength by leveraging remote Ryzen 7950X 16-core CPU servers and RTX 2080 GPUs for LcZero, providing deeper and faster analysis.
name: Next Chess Move

concept: **破门而入：行动小队 (Door Kickers: Action Squad):** 破门而入：行动小队 is a 2D tactical strategy shooter where players control one of six unique SWAT operators through 84 non-linear indoor levels, completing objectives like hostage rescue or bomb defusal. Players navigate the side-scrolling environment using a virtual joystick for horizontal movement, with dedicated on-screen buttons for actions such as jumping, shooting, breaching doors, and activating character-specific skills. Each of the six playable characters possesses unique weapon proficiencies and a skill tree for upgrades, allowing players to customize their loadout from over 60 weapons and equipment items. Progression involves completing missions against 20+ enemy types, unlocking new gear and abilities, and mastering tactical deployment to overcome increasing difficulty and complex level layouts. The game features additional modes like an "Endless Tower" with randomized challenges and a "Zombie Invasion" mode, with missions concluding successfully upon objective completion and failing if all operators are incapacitated.
name: 破门而入：行动小队
developer: Xiamen Kuaibaoyou Network Technology Co., Ltd

concept: **Pixel Cup Soccer - Ultimate:** Pixel Cup Soccer - Ultimate is a retro-style arcade soccer game where players control individual team members on a 2D pitch using a virtual joystick for movement and dedicated on-screen buttons for actions like short pass, long pass, and a charge-and-aim shot. Players can also perform slide tackles and direct corner kicks or throw-ins with precise timing and aiming, aiming to score more goals than the opposing AI or human team within a set match duration. Progression in Career Mode involves building a custom team, managing tactics via a tactical panel, and advancing through Leagues D, C, B, and A, as well as various cup competitions like the Global Cup. Difficulty scales through challenging AI opponents with distinct play styles (e.g., Catenaccio, Tiki-Taka) and the increasing stakes of higher-tier tournaments. Winning matches and tournaments is the primary objective, with losses impacting league standings and career progression.
name: Pixel Cup Soccer - Ultimate

concept: **时空之歌 (Song of Time):** 时空之歌 is a Roguelike turn-based strategy game where players command a clan of warriors on grid-based tactical combat maps, navigating procedurally generated "journeys" through a perilous wilderness. During combat, players select individual units by tapping them, then tap a grid square to move or an enemy/ally to target with one of hundreds of unique skills, with turn order dictating action sequence. Each of the dozens of clan members possesses distinct talents, and players can recruit beast armies, construct bronze colossi, or deploy magic arrays to counter varied enemy types across hundreds of unique terrain maps. Progression is run-based, with players aiming to complete a journey by overcoming increasingly difficult encounters and boss challenges, while meta-progression likely involves unlocking new clans or persistent upgrades that enhance future runs. A journey concludes upon the successful completion of all map objectives, or in defeat if the player's entire party is eliminated, necessitating a fresh start.
name: 时空之歌
