developer: Xiamen Daoqimao Network Technology Co., Ltd.

concept: **陰キャラブコメ (Yin Kyara Bukome)** This is a choice-driven visual novel where players progress through a narrative by tapping the screen to advance text and dialogue. Periodically, the game presents multiple-choice options, typically 2-4, which influence the protagonist's relationships with various "introverted" capture targets and branch the story into different narrative paths. Successfully navigating dialogue and making optimal choices accumulates unseen affection points with specific characters, leading towards one of several character-specific good endings or generic bad endings. The game features multiple distinct character routes, each requiring specific choices to unlock, and completion of all routes (estimated 3-6 hours total playtime) reveals the full story and unlocks a gallery of CGs and previously viewed scenes.
name: 陰キャラブコメ

concept: **우봉고 – 보드게임 그 자체 (Ubongo - The Board Game Itself)** Ubongo is a fast-paced spatial puzzle game where players are presented with a unique geometric board shape and a set of 3-5 polyomino tiles that must be precisely placed to completely fill the empty space. Players drag and drop tiles onto the board, tapping or using dedicated UI buttons to rotate and flip them until all pieces fit perfectly without overlap. In single-player Level Mode, difficulty scales across hundreds of levels by introducing more complex board shapes and tile configurations, with successful completion unlocking the next level and awarding 1-3 gems based on solve speed. The game also features a Free Mode with over 6,000 randomly generated puzzles and a Time Attack mode where players race against a 60-second timer to solve as many puzzles as possible, with the primary goal being to achieve the fastest solve times and earn the most gems.
name: 우봉고 – 보드게임 그 자체

concept: **My Diggy Dog** My Diggy Dog is an adventure platformer where players control Marty, a digging dog, through procedurally generated underground levels presented as a 2D side-scroller. Players use a virtual joystick on the left for movement and tap the right side of the screen to dig through dirt blocks, revealing paths, hidden treasures, and occasional enemies or environmental hazards. Marty has a limited stamina bar that depletes with digging and combat, requiring players to find food items to replenish it, and a health bar that decreases upon taking damage from traps or enemies. Progression involves collecting dozens of unique artifacts hidden throughout "hundreds of meters" of interconnected passages, completing quests from NPCs, and using collected coins to purchase or upgrade various digging gadgets and defensive items from a shop, ultimately aiming to uncover all secrets and complete the overarching narrative.
name: My Diggy Dog

concept: **Oddworld: Stranger's Wrath** Oddworld: Stranger's Wrath is a hybrid third-person platformer and first-person shooter where players control Stranger, a bounty hunter, navigating diverse environments using a virtual joystick for movement and a jump button. Combat seamlessly transitions to a first-person perspective upon aiming, where Stranger wields a unique dual-barreled crossbow that fires various live creatures as ammunition, each with distinct effects like stunning or poisoning enemies. Players must strategically capture outlaws by weakening them in combat and then "bagging" them using a dedicated UI button, earning Moolah currency which is used to purchase weapon upgrades, new ammo types, and health replenishments from vendors. The game features a linear story progression spanning over 20 hours, with difficulty scaling through increasingly challenging enemy encounters and boss fights, culminating in the completion of the main narrative and all major bounties.
name: Oddworld: Stranger's Wrath

concept: **命运女神：神与凡人 (Goddess of Destiny: Gods and Mortals)** Goddess of Destiny: Gods and Mortals is a multi-genre RPG where players embark on an epic quest across the Maia Continent, engaging in diverse gameplay mechanics depending on the region. The core loop involves navigating a world map, tapping on locations to initiate specific sub-games such as "push map" dungeon crawling in the Reins Empire, tower defense challenges in the Elf Kingdom, or exploration-based puzzles in the Talos Principality. Players manage a hero and a party of up to 3 recruited followers, each with distinct roles (e.g., tank, healer, DPS), whose stats and abilities are enhanced through a robust equipment system featuring random legendary drops, set bonuses, and a crafting system for enhancing, gemming, and smelting gear. Progression is driven by completing story quests, unlocking new hero job transfers with branching skill trees, and collecting 12 powerful artifacts, each with its own talent tree, ultimately aiming to defeat the demon lord and restore balance to the world.
name: 命运女神：神与凡人
developer: Wuhan Hurricane World Technology Co., Ltd.

concept: **OLO game** OLO is a turn-based digital board game for 2 or 4 players on a single device, where each player aims to get their colored OLO discs into designated target zones on the opposite side of the board. Players take turns launching their OLOs by dragging a finger back from their OLO to set power and direction, then releasing to flick it across the board. The board features a central neutral zone and two target zones per player, with players scoring 1 point for each of their OLOs that come to rest entirely within their target zone at the end of a round. A "death finger" mechanic allows players to swipe an opponent's OLO out of play if it lands in a specific penalty area, adding a strategic layer to disc placement. The game progresses through multiple rounds until all OLOs are played, with the player accumulating the most points winning, and new color themes unlocking after a set number of victories (e.g., 5 wins).
name: OLO game

concept: **무인도 생존 100일** This survival game challenges players to endure 100 days on a deserted island, managing various health metrics like hunger, thirst, vitality, and body temperature, which deplete over time at varying rates (e.g., hunger every 5 minutes). Players interact with the environment by tapping on resources like trees or rocks to gather materials, and on specific locations to move, with no travel time. Crafting new tools and furniture is central to progression, unlocking advanced recipes and structures, such as a fishing rod after crafting a basic axe, which then enables a fishing mini-game to acquire food. Health conditions like wounds or insect bites can occur randomly or from environmental hazards, requiring specific crafted remedies to prevent further health degradation. The game features dynamic weather, where rain might replenish thirst but reduce body temperature, forcing players to adapt their daily plans to survive until the 100-day mark.
name: 무인도 생존 100일

concept: **스왐피는 어디에** Players solve physics-based puzzles by swiping their finger across the screen to "cut" paths through dirt, guiding various types of liquid (fresh water, dirty water, toxic water, steam, sludge) to a specific destination. The primary objective in Swampy's story is to deliver a minimum volume of fresh water to his broken shower pipe, while avoiding obstacles and ensuring no toxic water reaches him. Each level features three rubber ducks strategically placed, which players must touch with the correct liquid type to collect, unlocking bonus levels and cosmetic items upon collecting a set number (e.g., 50 ducks). Progression involves completing over 500 increasingly complex levels across four distinct character stories, each introducing unique liquid types and environmental mechanics, such as steam that converts to water upon contact with cold surfaces. Failure occurs if insufficient water reaches the destination, the wrong liquid type is delivered, or the water is lost to the environment, requiring a level restart.
name: 스왐피는 어디에

concept: **Meridian 157: Chapter 2** Meridian 157 is a first-person point-and-click adventure game where players navigate static, pre-rendered environments by tapping on directional arrows to move between scenes and tapping on objects to interact with them. The core gameplay loop involves examining the environment for clues, collecting various inventory items by tapping them, and solving intricate puzzles that often require combining two or more items (e.g., using a key with a locked box) or manipulating environmental elements. Puzzles range from logic-based riddles to mechanical contraptions, with a logical hint system available to provide guidance after a set delay (e.g., 30 seconds) if a player is stuck. Progression is linear and story-driven, with players unlocking new areas and advancing the narrative by successfully solving a sequence of interconnected puzzles. The objective is to uncover the mystery of Meridian 157 and find an escape, with failure simply meaning the player cannot progress until the current puzzle is solved.
name: Meridian 157: Chapter 2

concept: **Layton : boîte de Pandore HD** Professor Layton and the Diabolical Box HD is a puzzle-adventure game where players guide Professor Layton and Luke through a narrative by tapping on the screen to move between locations and interact with characters and objects. The core gameplay involves discovering over 150 diverse puzzles, ranging from logic challenges and matchstick puzzles to trick questions, which are presented by NPCs or found hidden within the environment. Players solve these puzzles by tapping on screen elements to manipulate them or select answers, earning "Picarats" for correct solutions, with fewer Picarats awarded for using hints (up to 3 per puzzle). Progression is driven by the overarching mystery, as solving key puzzles unlocks new areas on the Molentary Express and advances the story, featuring more animated cutscenes than its predecessor. Additionally, three distinct mini-games, such as helping a hamster lose weight or brewing specific teas, provide optional challenges and unlock further content upon completion.
name: Layton : boîte de Pandore HD

concept: **Mini Airways:Premium**: Players act as an air traffic controller, tapping on planes to select them and then tapping on runways or destination points on a 2D airport map to assign takeoff, landing, or routing commands, with visual path lines indicating their trajectory. The core challenge involves continuously managing an increasing number of planes that spawn from off-screen entry points (e.g., every 5-10 seconds, decreasing to 2-3 seconds as difficulty scales), requiring precise timing to prevent any mid-air or ground collisions. Successfully guiding flights earns points and in-game currency, which is used to unlock 15 new global airports, each featuring unique runway configurations, and purchase over 10 types of airport upgrades that enhance efficiency or provide new control tools. The objective is to achieve the highest score by managing flights for the longest duration without any collisions, as any collision immediately results in a game over.
name: Mini Airways:Premium

concept: **Ticket to Earth**: Players engage in turn-based tactical combat on a grid-based battlefield, where character movement and actions are powered by matching colored tiles. On their turn, players tap and drag a path across adjacent, matching colored tiles on the grid to move a selected character, consuming those tiles and generating 'energy' for corresponding color-coded special abilities. Each character possesses unique abilities (e.g., attack, heal, buff) that require specific amounts of accumulated energy from matched tiles to activate, chosen by tapping the ability icon, with different tile colors often corresponding to different ability types or character stats. Completing over 120 story-driven missions awards experience points and currency, allowing players to level up characters, unlock new abilities from a skill tree, and equip various gear items that modify stats like health, damage, or energy generation. Missions have specific objectives, such as defeating all enemies or reaching an extraction point, and failure occurs if all player characters are defeated (HP reaches 0) or the mission objective is not met within a set turn limit.
name: Ticket to Earth

concept: **용사는 진행중 어드밴스드**: The hero automatically runs right and continuously performs basic attacks against enemies encountered in 12 distinct stages, while players actively manage combat through timed inputs. Players tap the 'Attack' button for standard attacks, or hold it to charge a powerful attack that releases upon lifting their finger, dealing increased damage and potentially breaking enemy defenses, while also tapping dedicated skill icons to unleash special abilities that consume mana. Enemies have varying health pools and attack patterns, with 12 unique bosses requiring specific strategies like breaking their shields with a charged attack before dealing significant damage, and players can use 6 distinct special items (e.g., 'Potion' to restore HP/MP, 'Bomb' for AoE damage) by tapping their respective icons during critical moments. Completing stages and defeating bosses awards gold and experience, used to upgrade the hero's stats (e.g., Attack, HP, Mana) and unlock new 'Artifacts' that provide passive bonuses, which can be swapped dynamically from the pause menu to adapt to different combat scenarios. The hero's health bar depletes upon taking damage, and the game ends if HP reaches zero, requiring players to restart the current stage or 'Reincarnate' to gain permanent bonuses, with the goal being to clear all 12 stages and defeat the final boss.
name: 용사는 진행중 어드밴스드

concept: **进化之地2**: Players control the protagonist, Kuro, through a 20-hour story campaign that seamlessly transitions between vastly different game genres and graphical styles across four distinct time eras. Gameplay dynamically shifts from a top-down JRPG perspective with directional pad controls for movement and a single 'Action' button for interactions and turn-based combat, to a side-scrolling platformer with 'Jump' and 'Attack' buttons, then to a 2D fighting game with specific combo inputs, and even puzzle or rhythm game segments, each with tailored control schemes. Advancing the narrative unlocks new eras, each introducing unique gameplay mechanics and visual aesthetics (e.g., 8-bit pixel art, 16-bit 2D, early 3D), while completing quests and defeating enemies awards experience points to level up Kuro and acquire new abilities relevant to the current genre. Difficulty arises from mastering the diverse control schemes and mechanics of each genre, with combat encounters featuring various enemy types and 14 unique bosses, some requiring specific genre-appropriate strategies to defeat. The game progresses by completing story objectives, solving puzzles, and defeating enemies, with failure typically resulting from Kuro's health reaching zero during combat or failing specific mini-game objectives, requiring a restart from the last checkpoint.
name: 进化之地2

concept: **Dentures and Demons**: Players navigate Detective Junior Peexelated through a pixelated point-and-click graphic adventure, exploring various scenarios by tapping on objects and characters to interact, gather clues, and advance the story across 6 chapters. Movement is achieved by tapping on desired locations within the scene, while interacting with objects or characters involves tapping them to trigger dialogue, examine items, or add them to an inventory accessible via a dedicated UI icon. Progression requires solving environmental puzzles by combining inventory items, using specific items on scene elements, or deducing solutions from dialogue, occasionally interspersed with reflex-based arcade mini-games that demand precise tap or swipe inputs to overcome. Successfully completing puzzles and mini-games unlocks new areas and advances the narrative through the game's 6 chapters, with hidden achievements discoverable by performing specific actions or finding secret items. Failure in arcade mini-games or making incorrect choices in critical puzzles may result in a temporary game over, requiring a retry from a recent checkpoint, with the ultimate goal being to uncover dark secrets and complete the overarching mystery.
name: Dentures and Demons

concept: **Brainess - Train your Brain:** Brainess is a collection of 7 distinct mini-games designed to test cognitive skills, accessed from a central menu via taps. Each mini-game, such as "Dice" (tap dice in ascending order from 1-6) or "Memory" (tap to flip cards and match 8 pairs), presents a series of challenges that require quick thinking and precise tapping. Players complete daily sessions across multiple mini-games, where performance is measured by accuracy and speed, contributing to a "Brain Age" score that decreases with improved results. Difficulty scales within each mini-game by increasing the number of elements (e.g., more dice, larger memory grids up to 6x6) or reducing time limits for responses, pushing players to achieve higher scores. The core loop involves daily engagement to improve individual mini-game scores and collectively lower the displayed "Brain Age," with no explicit unlockables beyond personal performance tracking.
name: Brainess - Train your Brain

concept: **萌妖出没-全新挂机鬼服奇幻仙侠手游:** This Q-version idle RPG features automated combat where the player's character and collected "萌妖" (cute monsters) continuously battle enemies in various fantasy zones without direct input. Players navigate the game's menus by tapping on icons to manage character equipment, upgrade monster companions, and assign skill points, with combat results and resource gains automatically displayed. Progression is primarily driven by accumulated offline rewards and active menu management, allowing players to level up their character and monsters, unlock new skills every 5 levels, and equip higher rarity gear (Common, Rare, Epic, Legendary). New adventure zones and challenging "神秘副本" (mystery dungeons) become accessible upon reaching specific character levels (e.g., Level 10 for first dungeon), requiring a minimum "战斗力" (combat power) score to successfully clear and earn unique item drops. The core loop involves strategic upgrades and resource allocation to continuously increase combat power, enabling further automated progression through increasingly difficult content.
name: 萌妖出没-全新挂机鬼服奇幻仙侠手游

concept: **Is This Yours?:** "Is This Yours?" is a fast-paced item-matching game where players manage a Lost & Found counter, receiving a continuous stream of unique items and impatient customers. Players must quickly identify the correct customer for each lost item by tapping the item and then tapping the corresponding customer, or by dragging the item directly to them, within a strict time limit per customer. In Survival mode, incorrect matches or exceeding a customer's patience meter results in losing 1 of 3 available hearts, with the game ending upon losing all hearts; successful matches award reputation points and contribute to a high score. Difficulty escalates as more items appear simultaneously (up to 5 on the counter), customer patience meters deplete faster, and new, more complex item types are introduced over time. The core loop involves rapidly processing customer requests to maximize reputation and extend the run, with a separate ZEN mode offering a pressure-free experience without hearts or time limits.
name: Is This Yours?

concept: **KAMI:** KAMI is a minimalist puzzle game where players are presented with a grid of colored paper segments and must transform the entire screen into a single, uniform color. The core mechanic involves tapping a colored segment, which then changes that segment and all adjacent, contiguous segments of the *same original color* to the newly selected color, effectively "folding" paper. The objective for each of the 72 unique puzzles is to achieve the target uniform screen color using the fewest possible moves, with a move counter tracking player actions. Progression is linear, with players unlocking the next puzzle only after successfully completing the current one, and a "perfect" score is achieved by matching or beating the optimal move count for each puzzle. Difficulty scales through increasingly complex initial paper patterns and larger grids, requiring more strategic color choices to minimize moves, with no time limits or failure states beyond exceeding the optimal move count.
name: KAMI

concept: **アンリアルライフ (Unreal Life):** アンリアルライフ is a pixel-art adventure game where players control Hal, an amnesiac girl, navigating a mysterious world by tapping on the screen to move her. Hal possesses the unique ability to "read memories" from objects by tapping them, which reveals a past version of that object or scene, often providing crucial clues for puzzles. Puzzles are solved by comparing the present state of the environment with its past memory, requiring players to interact with objects in a specific sequence or use information from the past to manipulate the present. Progression involves exploring new areas, engaging in dialogue with NPCs like the talking traffic light, and solving environmental puzzles to advance the narrative and uncover fragments of Hal's lost memories. The game features a branching narrative with 4 distinct endings, determined by specific player choices and actions made throughout the journey, encouraging multiple playthroughs.
name: アンリアルライフ

concept: **Legacy - Reawakening:** Players navigate a 3D environment by tapping on the screen to move to specific points of interest or interact with objects, and swipe to rotate the camera for exploration. The core gameplay loop involves discovering intricate mechanical contraptions, logic challenges, and visual puzzles, which are solved by collecting inventory items, combining them, and using them on environmental elements or other inventory items. Progression is tied to solving these unique puzzles, each unlocking new areas within the vast cave system and revealing narrative clues towards rebuilding a broken robot guardian by collecting its lost memory shards. Difficulty scales as puzzles become more complex, requiring multi-step solutions and keen observation, with a dynamic hint system available for assistance. The game concludes upon successfully rebuilding the guardian and uncovering the overarching mystery of the ancient ruins.
name: Legacy - Reawakening

concept: **下一站江湖Ⅱ:** Players explore a vast, seamless 256-zhang open world Wuxia environment using a virtual joystick for movement and on-screen buttons for combat and interaction, with camera control via swiping. The core loop involves engaging in "no rules" combat by freely combining hundreds of internal arts, external martial arts, and light body techniques, alongside developing various life skills like mining or forging to enhance character abilities. Progression is driven by player choices that create "causal chains," influencing narrative outcomes across 20+ endings and determining affiliations with various factions, each offering unique storylines and "simulated management" or "world conquest" mechanics. Difficulty scales through increasingly challenging combat encounters, complex faction rivalries, and hidden sandbox events that reward exploration and skill mastery. The game emphasizes high freedom, allowing players to forge their own path in the Jianghu, from a martial arts master to a merchant, with character growth and story advancement intertwined with every decision.
name: 下一站江湖Ⅱ
developer: Chengdu Yimoxuan Network Technology Co., Ltd

concept: **Brass:** Players compete as entrepreneurs in an economic strategy board game, interacting with the game board by tapping to select actions and dragging to place industry tiles or connection links. The core loop involves building industries like Cotton Mills or Coal Mines in specific towns, then constructing canals in the Canal Era and later railways in the Rail Era to connect these towns and facilitate trade. Progression occurs across these two eras, each with distinct action phases and scoring opportunities, where players develop their industries by spending money to upgrade their efficiency and victory point potential. Difficulty scales through strategic competition against 2-3 opponents (AI or human), requiring careful management of resources, income, and market prices for coal and iron to maximize production and sales. The game concludes after the Rail Era, with the player accumulating the highest victory points from their built industries, connections, and developed technology declared the winner.
name: Brass

concept: **농장 스토리:** Players manage a farm by tapping on plots to plant various crops like lettuce and strawberries, and on available land to construct tourist facilities such as campsites and gyms. The core loop involves cultivating crops and raising animals to produce "brand vegetables" and specialty products, which are then sold to fulfill shipment requests from stores and used to win contests, increasing farm reputation. Progression is achieved by expanding the farm's land, unlocking new crop types and animal breeds, and building more diverse tourist attractions to draw in increasing numbers of visitors, thereby boosting income. Difficulty scales as players must efficiently manage farm layout, balance production with demand, and strategically invest in facilities to maintain profitability and attract a target number of tourists. The ultimate goal is to transform a vast plot into a thriving, popular tourist farm, continually expanding and optimizing operations.
name: 농장 스토리

concept: **Tap Knight - Idle Adventure:** Players control a knight who automatically attacks enemies, while players can tap anywhere on the screen to deal additional damage, progressing through 200 levels across 10 unique worlds. The core loop involves defeating waves of monsters to earn experience and gold, which are then used to unlock and upgrade 20 unique skills within a customizable skill tree, allowing for varied combat strategies. Progression is continuous, as the game collects idle experience even when closed, enabling players to return stronger and overcome previously challenging boss fights that occur every 20 levels. Difficulty scales by increasing enemy health, damage output, and spawn rates per level, requiring strategic skill point allocation and the discovery of new allies to advance through the increasingly formidable monster hordes. The ultimate objective is to clear all 200 levels and defeat the final boss, defending the lost kingdom.
name: Tap Knight - Idle Adventure

concept: **The Longing Mobile:** Players control the Shade by tapping on the environment to direct its movement to specific locations or interact with objects like resources and books within a vast, hand-drawn underground cave system. The primary mechanic is a real-time 400-day countdown that progresses even when the app is closed, serving as the ultimate game timer for the king's awakening. Progression involves exploring the slowly revealed map, collecting specific items to furnish the Shade's living space, and reading books that pass in-game time faster (e.g., 1 hour of reading might advance certain time-based events by 2 hours). Time-based environmental puzzles require waiting for specific real-time durations (e.g., 24 hours for a mushroom to grow), and the Shade moves at a deliberately slow speed, approximately 1 unit per second, emphasizing patience. The game has multiple narrative endings determined by the player's cumulative actions and discoveries over the 400 days, with no traditional "loss" state, only different narrative outcomes upon the king's awakening.
name: The Longing Mobile

concept: **Machinarium:** Players control the robot Josef by tapping on interactive hotspots within static, hand-drawn scenes to move him, examine objects, or initiate dialogue with other characters. The core mechanic involves collecting various inventory items by tapping them, which are then stored in a persistent inventory accessible via a tap on an icon, and can be combined or dragged onto environmental elements to solve logic puzzles. Josef possesses a unique ability to extend or retract his body, allowing him to reach higher or lower interactive points, controlled by swiping up or down on the character model. Progression is linear, driven by successfully solving a series of interconnected logic and environmental puzzles, where each solution unlocks access to new areas or advances the narrative towards rescuing Berta. Difficulty scales with the complexity of multi-step puzzles and the abstract nature of item combinations, with no explicit health or time limits, and the game concludes upon successfully completing the final puzzle and saving Berta.
name: Machinarium (머시나리움)

concept: **Concordia: Digital Edition:** Players take turns selecting and playing one of their unique action cards from a hand of seven, which dictates available actions such as moving colonists, building trading posts, producing goods, or acquiring new cards. Players tap on the map to deploy colonists to cities via land or sea routes, then tap again to build trading posts in those cities, which consume specific resources (e.g., 2 bricks for a land post) from their limited storage capacity of 12 goods. Progression is driven by accumulating victory points (VP) throughout the game, awarded for built trading posts, collected goods, and the specific types of Persona cards acquired, with the game ending when a player builds their 15th trading post or the card deck runs out. Difficulty scales through strategic interaction with 2-5 AI opponents or human players, requiring careful resource management and anticipating opponents' moves to optimize card plays and maximize end-game VP. The UI features a central game board, player-specific resource and card displays, and a turn-based action log, where players tap cards to activate effects and drag resources or colonists to target locations.
name: Concordia: Digital Edition

concept: **VORAZ - Zombie survival:** Players control a character in a 3D environment using a virtual joystick on the left for movement and a virtual button on the right to fire their equipped weapon, aiming by dragging a finger across the screen. In EXPEDITION mode, players navigate a map to locate and collect valuable loot from designated points, engaging in real-time combat against AI-controlled zombies and potentially other player-controlled survivors, with the objective to reach an extraction zone before a 5-minute timer expires. In HORDE mode, players defend a fixed position against progressively difficult waves of infected, where each successfully cleared wave (e.g., 10 zombies) triggers an aerial supply drop containing health packs and ammunition, and the game ends upon player death. Progression involves earning in-game currency from successful extractions or horde rounds, which can be used to unlock and upgrade new weapons (e.g., increasing damage by 10% per level) and armor from a persistent inventory system. Difficulty scales in EXPEDITION with increased enemy density (e.g., 5-10 zombies per minute spawn rate) and stronger enemy types, and in HORDE by increasing the number and health points (e.g., +20 HP per wave) of zombies per wave.
name: VORAZ - Zombie survival

concept: **SAMURAI SHODOWN II:** Players control one of 15 unique sword-wielding characters in a 2D fighting arena, using a virtual D-pad on the left for movement (walk, jump, crouch) and a set of virtual buttons on the right (Light Slash, Medium Slash, Heavy Slash, Kick, SP for special moves) to execute attacks and defensive maneuvers. Combat involves precise timing of attacks, blocks, and dodges (Forward Somersault, Backward Flip) to deplete the opponent's 100 health points within a 99-second round timer. A "Rage Gauge" fills as the player takes damage or lands attacks, and upon reaching MAX capacity, allows the activation of a powerful, temporary damage boost and a single, unblockable Weapon Smash Attack. Progression in Arcade mode involves defeating a series of 10 AI-controlled opponents of increasing difficulty, culminating in a final boss battle, while Versus mode allows direct competition against another player via Wi-Fi multiplayer. The game features a best-of-three round system, with the winner being the first to secure two rounds, and a loss condition occurs when a character's health reaches zero or the round timer expires with less health than the opponent.
name: SAMURAI SHODOWN II

concept: **Vampire — Night Road:** This is a text-based interactive horror novel where players make narrative choices by tapping on presented text options, guiding their elite vampire courier character through a branching story. Players manage their character's Hunger by choosing to hunt, and must carefully maintain the Masquerade by avoiding actions that reveal their vampiric nature, as breaking it leads to destruction by fellow vampires or the Second Inquisition. Progression involves unlocking and utilizing various Disciplines like changing form or dominating minds, and modifying their car for specific attributes like speed or smuggling capacity, which influence the success of chosen actions in encounters. The core loop involves accepting delivery missions, making strategic choices to outrun hunters and rivals, and completing objectives before dawn, with the ultimate goal of delivering secrets and surviving the night to exploit the situation. Failure occurs if the character is destroyed by enemies, breaks the Masquerade, or fails to deliver their parcel by its implied expiration date.
name: Vampire — Night Road

concept: **日本事故物件監視協会 -JSP-:** Players act as a night monitor, observing surveillance camera feeds from real-world "accident properties" between 0:00 AM and 5:00 AM. The core mechanic involves actively scanning camera views for anomalies such as human figures, bloodstains, or video noise, and switching between normal and night vision camera modes by tapping on corresponding UI elements. Upon detecting an anomaly, players must promptly tap a "report" button to log the event, with the game's difficulty scaling as the frequency and subtlety of these phenomena increase over the 5-hour shift. The primary objective is to successfully monitor the property until 5:00 AM without accumulating too many reporting omissions or mistakes, which lead to a loss state. Progression is implied through surviving consecutive shifts, potentially unlocking access to new properties or more challenging monitoring scenarios.
name: 日本事故物件監視協会 -JSP-

concept: **Funny Ghosts! Games for kids:** The primary game mode is a "whack-a-mole" style experience where players tap on up to 30 different ghosts as they quickly peek out from various openings within a scary castle environment. Success in catching ghosts awards candies and scary cake, which serve as currency for progression, potentially unlocking additional mini-games or cosmetic items. The game also includes various mini-games, such as a witch-controlled avoidance game where players swipe to steer a witch away from incoming ghosts, and a memory matching game where players tap pumpkins to reveal and pair hidden monster cards. Difficulty scales by increasing the speed at which ghosts appear and retreat in the main game, or by introducing more complex patterns in mini-games. The objective is to achieve high scores by catching as many ghosts as possible within a time limit, or by completing mini-game objectives, fostering attention and reaction speed.
name: Funny Ghosts! Games for kids

concept: **Foretales:** Foretales is a story-driven, turn-based card game where players control Volepain and companions, navigating a branching narrative by playing cards from their deck to resolve encounters. Players manage resources and resolve conflicts through strategic card plays, choosing between diplomacy, stealth, or direct combat options presented on cards, with each choice impacting the unfolding story and available paths. Progression involves advancing through multiple storylines and making meaningful decisions that shape the fate of kingdoms, leading to various endings and encouraging replayability. The core loop consists of drawing cards, evaluating tactical situations, playing specific cards to influence outcomes, and then observing the narrative consequences, all without randomness or grind. The objective is to strategically utilize a curated deck to overcome challenges and guide the story towards a desired outcome, ultimately forging a specific destiny for the world.
name: Foretales

concept: **Super Farming Boy:** Super Farming Boy blends action, puzzle, and farming simulation, centered on the player character's ability to instantly transform into various tools like a shovel or pickaxe with a single tap of a UI button. The core gameplay revolves around strategically planting and harvesting magical seed creatures, which trigger powerful chain reactions and combos across the farm, efficiently clearing obstacles, collecting resources, and defending against creatures and seasonal bosses. Players utilize drag-and-drop touch controls for planting and interacting, and can also fly across the map using directional swipes, while managing daily stamina usage. Progression involves collecting and upgrading SuperTool trading cards to enhance abilities, rescuing idle helper pets that automate tasks like auto-watering, and earning enough currency from harvests to buy back captured friends and mom. The objective is to master chain reactions across diverse seasons like Volcanic or Radioactive, defeat bosses, and ultimately accumulate sufficient resources to achieve the rescue goal.
name: Super Farming Boy

concept: **Hoplite:** Hoplite is a turn-based tactical roguelike played on small, hexagonal grid maps, where players tap on adjacent tiles to move their Hoplite character or tap on an enemy within attack range to initiate combat. Each turn, the player performs one action (move, attack, or use an ability), after which all enemies on the map simultaneously perform their actions according to their AI patterns, such as moving 1-2 tiles towards the player or attacking. Progression in Quest Mode involves descending through procedurally generated dungeon levels, with players earning "Fervor" by defeating enemies, which can be spent at altars to acquire one of three randomly offered permanent ability upgrades (e.g., +1 spear range, +1 shield block, +1 jump distance). The game ends upon the player's health reaching zero, with the objective being to delve as deep as possible into the dungeon, while difficulty scales by increasing enemy density, variety, and health points every 5 levels. Missions, like "defeat 10 archers" or "reach depth 5 without taking damage," unlock new starting upgrades or character variants for subsequent runs.
name: Hoplite

concept: **Tsuro - El Juego del Camino:** Tsuro is a digital board game where 2-8 players take turns placing path tiles onto a 6x6 grid board to guide their dragon stone along a continuous path. On their turn, a player selects one of three randomly drawn path tiles from their hand, rotates it by tapping, and then drags it onto an empty space adjacent to their dragon stone, immediately moving their stone along the newly formed path. The core game loop involves strategically placing tiles to extend one's own path while simultaneously attempting to redirect or force opponents' stones off the board or into a loop, as any stone leaving the board or entering a closed loop is eliminated. The game ends when only one player's stone remains on the board or all players are trapped in loops, with the objective being to be the last player remaining, and difficulty against AI opponents is adjusted by selecting one of three distinct AI personalities. Achievements, unlocked for specific feats like "survive 10 turns" or "eliminate 3 opponents," track player mastery and contribute to a "Master the Dragon" meta-progression after 50 achievements.
name: Tsuro - El Juego del Camino

concept: **Raiden Legacy:** Raiden Legacy is a classic vertical-scrolling shoot 'em up collection where players control a fighter jet, automatically firing forward, by dragging their finger across the screen in "First Touch" mode (ship follows finger) or by dragging relative to an initial tap in "Arcade Touch" mode (ship moves relative to touch). The core gameplay involves dodging dense patterns of enemy bullets and projectiles while destroying waves of enemy aircraft and large boss ships that appear at the end of each stage. Players collect power-ups dropped by specific enemies, such as "P" for weapon upgrades (increasing shot spread or damage) and "M" for missile upgrades (adding homing or straight-firing secondary weapons), with weapon levels resetting upon player death. Progression occurs through "Mission Mode," where players unlock and replay individual stages after completing them in "Arcade Mode," and difficulty can be set to "Medium" or "Hard," influencing enemy health, bullet speed, and spawn rates. The game features 15+ distinct aircraft, each with unique weapon types and a "Charge Shot" ability activated by holding the screen, which can be unlocked by achieving specific scores or completing certain missions.
name: Raiden Legacy

concept: **STEINS;GATE KR (한국어):** STEINS;GATE KR is a visual novel where players primarily advance the narrative by tapping the screen to read dialogue and descriptive text, with a total playtime exceeding 30 hours across multiple routes. The core mechanic is the "Phone Trigger System," where the player's in-game mobile phone acts as a decision-making interface; holding the device vertically brings up the phone to send or receive emails, while holding it horizontally puts it away. The narrative branches and character relationships are influenced by the content and timing of these phone interactions, such as choosing to reply to a specific email or ignoring a call, leading to different plot developments and one of several distinct endings for the 6 main characters. Progression involves replaying the story to explore alternative phone choices and unlock new narrative paths, aiming to discover all possible endings and understand the full scope of the time-traveling plot. The game's difficulty lies in discerning the subtle impact of each phone interaction on the story's trajectory, as seemingly minor choices can drastically alter the outcome.
name: STEINS;GATE KR (한국어)

concept: **ソニック・ザ・ヘッジホッグ (Sonic the Hedgehog):** Sonic the Hedgehog is a fast-paced 2D platformer where players control Sonic (or unlockable characters Tails and Knuckles) using a virtual D-pad for left/right movement and a dedicated jump button, with a second tap in mid-air initiating a spin-dash or character-specific ability like Tails' flight. The core gameplay involves navigating through 7 distinct "Zones," each comprising 3 acts and a boss battle, by running, jumping, and spinning through loop-de-loops and obstacles while collecting golden rings that act as a health buffer (losing all rings upon damage, but not dying unless hit with zero rings). Progression is achieved by completing each Zone, culminating in a final boss encounter, and players can unlock Tails (who can fly for 5 seconds) and Knuckles (who can glide and climb walls) after completing the main story once, offering new ways to explore levels. Difficulty scales through increasingly complex level layouts, faster enemy projectiles, and more intricate boss attack patterns across the 7 Zones, with additional challenge modes like "Time Attack" and "Boss Attack" encouraging speedrunning and mastery.
name: ソニック・ザ・ヘッジホッグ

concept: ELOH: ELOH is a musical puzzle game where players manipulate "spirit" blocks on a static grid to guide a rhythmic sound wave from a source to a target. Players use intuitive tap-and-drag gesture controls to reposition spirits, each possessing a unique reflective property and emitting a distinct musical note when struck by the wave. The core game loop involves experimenting with spirit placement to create a continuous path and a harmonious musical sequence, with success achieved when the sound wave reaches the target, completing the level's rhythmic pattern. Progression occurs linearly through 98 handcrafted levels, with new spirit types and increasingly complex grid layouts introduced to gradually escalate the puzzle difficulty without time limits or failure states beyond not solving the puzzle.
name: ELOH

concept: DRAGON QUEST BUILDERS: DRAGON QUEST BUILDERS is a block-building action RPG where players explore a voxel-based world, gathering resources, crafting items, and constructing structures to rebuild civilization. Players tap the screen to place blocks on a 3D grid and use specialized cursors to destroy environmental blocks, collecting raw materials like stone and wood. The core loop involves venturing into the world to gather materials, crafting essential tools, weapons, and building components at workbenches, then returning to a designated area to construct and expand a base or town. Combat against various Dragon Quest monsters occurs in real-time using crafted weapons, with player health points decreasing upon taking damage and requiring healing items. Progression is chapter-based, requiring players to complete specific quests like attracting 5 new NPCs or building 3 specific room types, unlocking new crafting recipes and areas as they advance towards defeating the main antagonist.
name: DRAGON QUEST BUILDERS

concept: Tiny Dangerous Dungeons: Tiny Dangerous Dungeons is a 2D Metroidvania platformer where players control Timmy, navigating a large, interconnected dungeon map presented in a monochrome Game Boy aesthetic. Players move Timmy using a virtual D-pad for left/right movement, tap a dedicated button to jump, and another to perform a basic melee attack against enemies. The core gameplay loop involves exploring rooms, defeating enemies that typically have 1-2 health points, and solving environmental puzzles to find hidden ability upgrades, such as a double jump or a ranged projectile, which unlock access to previously inaccessible areas. Timmy has 3 health points, and losing all health from enemy damage or environmental hazards results in respawning at the last checkpoint, while progression is achieved by acquiring all 5-8 key abilities to fully explore the dungeon and reach the final treasure.
name: Tiny Dangerous Dungeons

concept: Sync: Party Hard: Sync: Party Hard is a unique puzzle game where players must physically synchronize multiple touch inputs to align cosmic elements and "put the universe back together." Each puzzle presents a fragmented image or pattern on screen, requiring players to simultaneously place and hold 2-5 fingers on specific, glowing nodes to complete the circuit or align the pattern. The core game loop involves analyzing the required finger positions, contorting one's hand or body to reach all necessary nodes, and maintaining simultaneous contact for a brief duration, typically 1-3 seconds, to successfully "sync" the elements. Progression occurs across numerous levels, with difficulty scaling by increasing the number of required touch points, reducing the allowed contact area for each finger, or introducing moving targets that demand precise timing and coordination. Failure to maintain all required contacts simultaneously, or exceeding a 5-second time limit for each attempt, resets the puzzle.
name: Sync: Party Hard
developer: Siis Bilisim Teknoloji Ticaret ve Pazarlama Ltd. Sti.

concept: Goat Simulator PAYDAY: Goat Simulator PAYDAY is an open-world sandbox action game where players control one of four animal "thugs," each possessing unique physics-defying abilities, to complete chaotic heists. Players navigate the environment using a virtual joystick for movement, with dedicated buttons for jumping, performing a headbutt/lick action, and activating their character's special ability (e.g., the flamingo's flight or the dolphin's wheelchair wall-climb). The core game loop involves selecting missions from the "PRANKNET" menu, which typically require players to cause specific destruction, steal 3-5 designated items, or transport objects to a drop-off point while evading a dynamic police pursuit system that escalates from 1 to 5 wanted stars. Successfully completing heists earns in-game cash, which is used to unlock 14 unique "mutators" that alter gameplay physics or character attributes, and various cosmetic masks. The game features no traditional loss state, instead focusing on player-driven chaos and objective completion, with police simply incapacitating the player temporarily upon capture.
name: Goat Simulator PAYDAY

concept: Tichu is a four-player partnership card game where two teams of two compete to be the first to play all cards from their hand. Players interact by tapping and dragging cards from their hand at the bottom of the screen to the central play area, forming valid combinations like single cards, pairs, straights, or bombs, following the lead suit or combination. The core loop involves playing cards strategically to win tricks and empty one's hand, with the first player to empty their hand earning bonus points and the team with both players out first scoring significantly more. Before a hand begins, players can declare "Tichu" for 100 bonus points or "Grand Tichu" for 200 points if they believe they will be the first to play all their cards, adding a high-stakes risk-reward element to each round. Difficulty for AI opponents is adjustable through in-game settings, offering varying levels of strategic play, and overall progression is measured by accumulating points across multiple hands to determine the winning partnership.
name: Tichu

concept: Keep Talking & Nobody Explodes is a cooperative puzzle game where one player, the Defuser, interacts with a procedurally generated bomb displayed on-screen, while other players, the Experts, consult an external Bomb Defusal Manual (physical printout or separate device) to provide instructions. The Defuser uses taps and swipes to manipulate various modules on the bomb, such as cutting wires, pressing buttons, or deciphering symbols, under a strict time limit typically ranging from 5 to 10 minutes per bomb. Experts must verbally communicate specific instructions to the Defuser based on the bomb's visual details, which they cannot see, and the Defuser must accurately describe the modules to the Experts. Each bomb features 3-6 distinct modules, and incorrect actions or running out of time results in a bomb explosion, ending the round. Progression occurs through a Mission mode, introducing new, more complex modules and tighter time constraints across 20+ missions, or players can customize bombs in Free Play mode by selecting specific module counts and time limits.
name: Keep Talking & Nobody Explodes

concept: Doodle Kingdom is a two-part game featuring a core element-combining puzzle and a secondary runner combat mode. In the puzzle mode, players drag and drop elemental icons from a palette onto each other, such as "Water" onto "Earth" to create "Swamp," to discover new elements and populate their fantasy kingdom. Successfully combining elements unlocks new categories and elements, with over 100 unique combinations to discover, progressing through different eras of kingdom building. The secondary "runner" combat mode involves a Knight character automatically running forward, where players tap the screen to attack incoming monsters or swipe up to jump over obstacles, aiming to defeat 5-10 enemies per stage and a boss at the end of each zone. Progression in the runner mode involves collecting gold from defeated enemies to upgrade the Knight's armor and weapons, increasing attack damage by 5-10% per upgrade level, and unlocking new combat abilities.
name: Doodle Kingdom™

concept: Dungeon and Puzzles is a turn-based, grid-based puzzle game where players control a character navigating 150 handcrafted 2D dungeon rooms, moving one tile at a time using directional swipes or on-screen arrow buttons. The core mechanic involves manipulating the environment and enemies using specific tools: players can tap an adjacent monster to attack with a sword, tap a distant enemy to fire an arrow (consuming one arrow per shot), swipe into a block to push it, or tap a special glove icon then an enemy to pull it one tile closer. Each room presents a unique puzzle requiring players to reach an exit tile, often by clearing all enemies or moving specific blocks, with failure occurring if the player is cornered or defeated by monsters (each monster typically having 1-3 health points). Progression involves clearing rooms on a non-linear dungeon map, and players are challenged to achieve "advanced goals" by completing rooms within a par number of moves, encouraging replayability and optimized solutions.
name: Dungeon and Puzzles

concept: Chess Pro is a digital implementation of the classic board game, allowing players to move chess pieces on an an 8x8 grid using tap-and-drag gestures, adhering to standard chess rules for movement, capture, and special moves like castling or en passant. Players can engage in single-player matches against a configurable AI with 25 distinct difficulty levels, ranging from beginner to grandmaster, or compete in local two-player mode on a single device. The game features a "Coach" mode that provides real-time feedback, highlighting suboptimal moves or suggesting better opening strategies, aiding player skill development. Progression is primarily skill-based, measured by advancing through AI difficulty levels and solving a variety of pre-set chess problems and tactical puzzles, which challenge players to find checkmate in a specific number of moves or win material. Online multiplayer is also available via Game Center, allowing players to compete against others in live matches with various timer settings, such as 5-minute blitz games.
name: 체스 Pro
developer: The Chess Solitaire Sudoku Factory Ltd

concept: **던전 워페어 2**: Dungeon Warfare 2 is a tower defense game where players strategically place 33 unique traps, each with 8 distinct traits, onto a fixed dungeon path to eliminate waves of 30+ enemy adventurers and 5+ bosses. Players interact by tapping to select a trap from their inventory and then tapping on an available grid tile along the path to deploy it, aiming to utilize physics-based interactions to maximize damage and crowd control. Successful defense of waves awards experience points, which are used to unlock passive trap upgrades and allocate skill points across 3 skill trees, each containing 11 active and passive abilities, alongside earning currency to acquire 20+ unique equipment pieces. Difficulty scales across 60 handcrafted levels and infinite procedural dungeons, with players able to combine difficulty runes to customize challenges for increased rewards. A run ends if any adventurer reaches the dungeon's core, requiring players to restart the level.
name: 던전 워페어 2

concept: **Killer Sudoku CTC**: Killer Sudoku CTC is a logic puzzle game where players fill a 9x9 grid with digits 1-9, ensuring each digit appears only once per row, column, and 3x3 subgrid. The core mechanic involves solving "cages," which are groups of cells outlined on the grid, where the sum of the digits within each cage must equal a specified number, providing additional logical constraints. Players interact by tapping a cell to select it, then tapping a number from a 1-9 keypad to input their guess, with an optional "pencil mark" mode for jotting down possibilities. Progression is achieved by solving puzzles, earning stars that unlock access to more of the 100 main puzzles and 15 bonus beginner puzzles, with difficulty carefully calibrated from easy to extreme. A puzzle is won when all cells are correctly filled according to all Sudoku and Killer Sudoku rules, with hints available to guide players through challenging deductions.
name: Killer Sudoku CTC

concept: **Amazing Frog? (ko)**: Amazing Frog? is an open-world sandbox game where players control a ragdoll frog using a virtual joystick for movement and on-screen buttons for actions like jumping, attacking, and interacting with objects and vehicles. Players explore the town of Swindon, utilizing parkour mechanics like bouncing on trampolines and launching from cannons, while engaging in activities such as driving various vehicles (cars, jets, boats) and using a range of weapons (pistols, bombs, melee items) against enemies like zombies and sharks. Progression involves completing quests like rescuing kittens or tidying the town to earn Swindon Citizen Points (SCP), and discovering brown portaloos to unlock new costumes or blue portaloos in the hideout for customization. The game features a phone UI with apps, including a spawn point app to teleport to previously visited orange portaloos after dying, encouraging continuous exploration and player-driven chaos without a definitive win condition.
name: Amazing Frog? (ko)

concept: **饥饿派画家2：迷失**: Passpartout 2: The Lost Artist is an art simulation game where players assume the role of a struggling artist, using touch controls to draw paintings on a canvas with various digital tools like brushes and crayons. The core loop involves creating artwork by swiping and tapping on the screen to apply colors and strokes, then selling these paintings to passersby in the town of Phoenix to earn in-game currency. Players progress by using earned money to purchase new art supplies and studio upgrades from an art shop, enhancing their creative capabilities and efficiency. Additionally, players interact with town residents, accepting commissions like drawing new advertisements for Steve's restaurant, which, upon successful completion, can unlock unique items to aid in future creations. The ultimate goal is to revive the artist's career by exploring Phoenix, fulfilling resident wishes, and eventually challenging the Master Museum, with financial mismanagement potentially leading to eviction.
name: 饥饿派画家2：迷失
developer: Xiamen Kuaibaoyou Network Technology Co., Ltd

concept: **Garten of Banban 7**: Garten of Banban 7 is a first-person horror-exploration game where players navigate the monster-occupied levels of Banban’s Kindergarten using a virtual joystick for movement and on-screen buttons for interacting with environmental elements like doors, objects, and clues. The core mechanic involves stealthily exploring the mysterious establishment, solving environmental puzzles to unlock new areas, and uncovering the truth behind the missing child while avoiding or escaping various hostile monster entities. Difficulty scales through increasingly dangerous environments and more aggressive enemy encounters as players delve deeper, with the objective being to survive and advance the narrative by reaching specific story points. A run ends if the player is caught or defeated by a monster, forcing a respawn from the last checkpoint, emphasizing careful navigation and resource management in a solo survival experience.
name: Garten of Banban 7

concept: **Dungeon Warfare 2:** Players act as a Dungeon Lord, strategically placing a selection of 33 unique traps, each with 8 distinct traits, within a dungeon layout to eliminate waves of over 30 unique enemy types. Traps are placed by tapping on available grid squares or designated points within the dungeon path, with players managing a resource pool to deploy and upgrade them during or between waves. The core loop involves observing enemy pathing, selecting appropriate traps from a UI menu, and activating environmental hazards, with physics-enabled traps creating dynamic interactions like pushing enemies into pits or crushing them. Progression occurs through earned experience points from successful defenses, which unlock passive upgrades for all traps and skill points to invest across three distinct skill trees, each containing 11 skills, alongside an equipment system featuring over 30 unique items. Difficulty scales by combining difficulty mode runes for increased challenge and rewards, and players win a level by eliminating all waves of adventurers before a set number of adventurers escape the dungeon exit, losing if too many escape.
name: Dungeon Warfare 2

concept: **Cluedo: Juego oficial Hasbro:** Players navigate a 3D digital recreation of the Tudor Mansion board by tapping to roll a virtual die and then selecting their character's movement path into one of the nine rooms. Upon entering a room, players make a "suggestion" by selecting a suspect, a weapon, and the current room from a UI menu, prompting other players to secretly refute it by showing a single matching card if they possess one, which is then automatically marked on the player's digital clue sheet. The core loop involves gathering information by making suggestions and observing which cards are shown or not shown, using this deduction to narrow down the possible murderer, weapon, and location. When confident, a player makes an "accusation" by selecting the final suspect, weapon, and room; if correct, they win the game, but an incorrect accusation results in immediate loss and removal from the current match. The game offers various modes, including "Ultimate Detective" where all players simultaneously and secretly respond to suggestions, and progression involves unlocking new cases, characters, and clue sets as additional content.
name: Cluedo: Juego oficial Hasbro

concept: **Snail Bob (Bob L'escargot):** Players guide the continuously forward-moving Snail Bob through 80 distinct puzzle levels by interacting with environmental elements via taps and swipes on the screen. The core mechanic involves observing Bob's path and timing interactions with objects like levers to open doors, buttons to activate platforms, or rotating gears to create safe passages, often requiring quick reflexes to prevent Bob from falling into pits or encountering hazards. Players can also tap a specific UI button to make Bob retract into his shell and stop moving, allowing for precise timing or observation before proceeding. Progression is tied to completing each level, which unlocks the next stage and new themed chapters like "dangerous construction site" or "ancient Egypt," while collecting three hidden stars per level unlocks bonus content such as humorous portraits and sculptures. The objective for each level is to safely guide Bob to the exit portal, with failure occurring if Bob collides with an enemy, falls off the screen, or is destroyed by an environmental trap.
name: Snail Bob (Bob L'escargot)

concept: **Dragon Hills 2 [드래곤 힐스2]:** Players control a mechanical dragon in a side-scrolling, physics-based action game using a single-touch control scheme: tapping and holding the screen makes the dragon dive underground, while releasing the tap causes it to jump out and slide along the surface. The core mechanic involves strategically timing dives and jumps to destroy fully destructible terrain elements like buildings, bridges, and vehicles, as well as to attack various enemies including zombies, giants, and other dragons encountered on the surface or underground. Progression is achieved by conquering 28 distinct towers spread across 4 unique worlds, earning currency or points that unlock and upgrade new weapons, different dragon types, and explosive power-ups, each offering distinct visual effects and combat advantages. The game features epic boss battles that require specific attack patterns to defeat, and the run continues until the dragon's health bar is depleted from taking damage from enemies or environmental hazards. The primary objective is to achieve the highest possible score by destroying obstacles and enemies, and progressing through the challenging worlds.
name: Dragon Hills 2 [드래곤 힐스2]

concept: **Unruly Heroes:** Players control a team of four distinct heroes in a 2D action-adventure platformer, navigating levels inspired by "Journey to the West" by using a virtual joystick for movement and dedicated on-screen buttons for jumping, attacking, and activating unique character abilities. The core mechanic involves seamlessly switching between the four heroes at any time via a dedicated UI button, utilizing each character's specific combat style and special skills to defeat various monsters, solve environmental puzzles, and overcome complex platforming challenges. Combat involves chaining basic attacks with special abilities, while puzzles often require a specific hero's power, such as one character's ability to double jump across a wide gap or another's strength to push a heavy block. Progression is achieved by completing levels and defeating bosses, which allows players to collect loot and potentially unlock new "holy relics" or upgrade existing character abilities, with the ultimate goal of reuniting the scattered scripture fragments. Failure occurs if all four heroes are defeated, indicated by their individual health bars depleting, requiring a restart from a checkpoint.
name: Unruly Heroes

concept: **迷失岛外传南瓜镇:** This is a point-and-click adventure game where players navigate static 2D scenes by tapping directional arrows or scene exits to move between locations. The core mechanic involves tapping on objects to inspect them, collecting inventory items, and interacting with characters to gather clues and initiate dialogue. Puzzles are solved by dragging inventory items onto other items to combine them, or by dragging them onto specific environmental hotspots, often requiring players to find 3-5 key items or clues per area to progress. The game progresses linearly through interconnected chapters, with difficulty scaling as puzzles become more abstract, involve more complex item combinations (e.g., 3-part items), and require careful observation of subtle environmental details. There are no traditional loss conditions; players win by solving all narrative-driven puzzles and completing the story, with progression gated by successful puzzle completion.
name: 迷失岛外传南瓜镇

concept: **Kingdomino: The Board Game:** This digital board game adaptation features 1-4 players taking turns drafting domino-like tiles from a central pool and strategically placing them into their personal 5x5 or 7x7 kingdom grid. Each tile consists of two squares with various terrain types (e.g., forest, lake), some containing crowns, and must be placed adjacent to a matching terrain type or the starting castle. The drafting mechanic dictates that selecting a higher-numbered, more desirable tile in the current round means picking later in the subsequent round, adding a layer of strategic turn order manipulation. At the game's conclusion, points are calculated by multiplying the number of connected squares in each contiguous territory by the number of crowns within that territory, with the highest score winning. Players interact by tapping to select tiles, then dragging and dropping them onto their grid, with a tap-to-rotate function for optimal placement, and can unlock over 80 cosmetic achievements like new castle skins or meeple designs.
name: Kingdomino: The Board Game

concept: **Final Frontier Story:** In this Kairosoft-style simulation game, players manage an intergalactic city by placing various buildings (e.g., residential, commercial, military) onto a grid-based map using drag-to-scroll and pinch-to-zoom controls. The core loop involves strategically placing buildings to optimize resource generation (e.g., money, research points) and resident satisfaction, aiming to boost the city's "Charm" rating. The game features a secondary combat loop where players dispatch fighter aircraft and trained pilots against alien monsters in turn-based battles, issuing commands by tapping units and selecting actions from a pop-up menu. Progression involves unlocking new building types, aircraft (e.g., 3 upgrade tiers for weapons), and pilot skills (e.g., max level 99) through research, with difficulty scaling as more challenging monsters appear requiring stronger units. The primary goal is to achieve the highest possible city rank and attract a diverse population, with no strict loss condition beyond inefficient management.
name: Final Frontier Story

concept: **Neighbours back From Hell:** This stealth puzzle game puts players in control of Woody, navigating multi-room environments (e.g., a house with 5 rooms) from an isometric 2D perspective by tapping on desired locations. The core mechanic involves finding specific items (e.g., banana peel, glue), combining them in the inventory (accessed via a bottom bar), and then placing the resulting prank in a precise location to trigger a comedic reaction from the neighbor. Players must execute 3-5 pranks per episode to achieve a target "TV rating" (e.g., 100%) without being detected by the neighbor or other NPCs who follow predictable patrol paths, as detection results in an immediate game over and episode restart. Successfully completing episodes unlocks new locations and 28 total episodes, with difficulty scaling through increased room complexity, additional patrolling NPCs, and more intricate, multi-step prank setups.
name: Neighbours back From Hell

concept: **スーパーブラックジャック2:** This is a pachislot machine simulator where players initiate spins by tapping a "Spin" button, then tap three individual "Stop" buttons (left, middle, right) to halt the reels and form winning symbol combinations across paylines. Winning combinations award in-game "medals" and can trigger various bonus games or "Stock Time" (ST) modes, which are periods of increased winning probability or guaranteed bonus payouts. Players can toggle an "Auto Play" feature with three speed settings (Normal, High Speed, Super High Speed) for automated gameplay, and utilize "Forced Flag" options to guarantee specific winning combinations or bonus triggers for testing purposes. The game allows selection of different "Pachislot settings" (e.g., Setting 1-6) that influence the underlying probability of hitting bonuses, with higher settings generally yielding better medal returns. The primary goal is to accumulate medals and experience the machine's various bonus features, with no traditional win/loss conditions beyond the player's choice to continue or stop.
name: スーパーブラックジャック2

concept: **The NOexistenceN of you AND me:** This is a psychological horror visual novel where players primarily interact by tapping the screen to advance dialogue and narrative text, occasionally presented with multiple-choice options (typically 2-4 per decision point) that dictate the story's progression. Players make hundreds of choices throughout the narrative, such as "Saludar a la chica" or "Responder sobre 'la existencia de la chica'," each leading to branching storylines and uncovering different facets of the protagonist's reality and the mysterious girl's identity. Progression involves unlocking new narrative paths and discovering multiple distinct endings, with certain choices leading to specific character interactions, surreal events, or critical plot revelations. The game's difficulty scales by presenting increasingly ambiguous or morally complex choices that significantly alter the narrative trajectory, encouraging replays to explore all possible outcomes and achieve a "true" or "complete" ending.
name: The NOexistenceN of you AND me

concept: **Daddy Was A Thief:** Players control a thief falling through a multi-story building, aiming to collect money and cause destruction while avoiding capture. The core mechanic involves swiping down to smash through the floor below, descending to a lower level, or swiping up to jump to the floor above, allowing for strategic movement across multiple vertical lanes. Each smashed object, like walls or furniture, awards points and money, contributing to a destruction combo meter that temporarily increases score multipliers and character speed. The game continuously scrolls downwards, with police and obstacles appearing at increasing frequencies and speeds on various floors, and the run ends when the player is caught by an enemy or falls off the screen. Collected money is used to unlock new playable thieves, each with unique visual designs, and various power-ups like temporary invincibility or coin magnets, enhancing subsequent runs.
name: Daddy Was A Thief

concept: **Wings of Glory:** Wings of Glory is a turn-based tactical strategy game adapting the tabletop aerial combat system, where players command squadrons of WWI biplanes in dogfights across a hexagonal grid. During each turn, players secretly select a maneuver card (e.g., "Straight 3," "Turn Left 2") for each of their planes, which are then revealed simultaneously, dictating movement across 1-3 hexes based on the card's numerical value and direction. Combat occurs when an enemy plane enters a player's firing arc and range (typically 1-2 hexes), prompting a damage roll where players draw damage cards from a deck, applying 1-5 damage points based on the card's value until a plane's 5-10 health points are depleted. Progression involves completing a series of historical scenarios and campaigns, unlocking new aircraft models with distinct maneuver decks and pilot abilities, with victory achieved by eliminating all enemy planes or fulfilling specific mission objectives.
name: Wings of Glory

concept: **Door Kickers: Action Squad:** Door Kickers: Action Squad is a 2D side-scrolling action game where players control a SWAT trooper, navigating levels by using a virtual joystick for left/right movement and tapping to fire their equipped weapon, managing recoil and limited ammunition. Players kick down doors by tapping them, engaging enemies with distinct behaviors and weapon types, utilizing cover by standing behind objects to reduce incoming damage by 50%. Strategic Abilities, such as deploying a health pack to restore 50 HP or calling for a gear refill, are activated by tapping on corresponding UI buttons, consuming earned "Strategic Points" accumulated by defeating enemies. Progression involves completing 84 non-linear levels across 4 mission objectives, earning XP to level up 6 unique characters and unlock over 60 weapons and gear items, while failure occurs if the player's health reaches zero.
name: Door Kickers: Action Squad

concept: **SpongeBob SquarePants: BfBB:** This 3D action-platformer allows players to control SpongeBob, Patrick, or Sandy, navigating linear levels by using a virtual joystick for movement and on-screen buttons for jumping, attacking, and character-specific abilities. SpongeBob can attack with his "Mighty Bubbles" dealing 10 damage per hit, Patrick can pick up and throw objects or enemies, and Sandy can use her lasso to swing across gaps or glide, each vital for overcoming specific environmental puzzles and combat encounters. Players progress by defeating Plankton's robot army, collecting "Shiny Objects" (the primary currency) and "Golden Spatulas" (key collectibles) scattered throughout the environment, with 10-15 Golden Spatulas typically required to unlock new hub areas or boss battles. The game features a health bar (typically 3-5 hit points) for each character, depleting upon contact with enemies or environmental hazards, with failure resulting in a respawn at the last checkpoint.
name: SpongeBob SquarePants: BfBB

concept: **Joe Danger:** Joe Danger is a stunt-based motorcycle platformer where players guide Joe through linear levels, automatically moving forward on a fixed path. Players tap to jump over gaps and obstacles, hold to crouch under barriers, swipe across Joe to perform a wheelie for bonus points, and use specific multi-touch gestures like pinching to clear certain path obstructions. Each level presents a series of environmental challenges, requiring precise timing and execution of these gestures to avoid crashing into obstacles like buses or giant mousetraps, and to collect scattered coins and stars. Progression involves completing over 20 hours of content across multiple levels, with new levels and over 25 unique characters unlocked by achieving specific score thresholds or collecting a set number of stars within each stage. Difficulty increases as levels introduce more complex obstacle sequences, tighter timing windows for stunts, and multi-stage challenges like wiggling to fight sharks, with a run ending upon any collision or failure to complete a required stunt.
name: Joe Danger

concept: **蒙特祖玛3 (Montezuma 3):** Montezuma 3 is a fast-paced match-3 puzzle game where players swap adjacent gems on a grid to form lines of three or more identical gems, clearing them and causing new gems to cascade down. Each of the 200 levels presents a 60-second time limit, challenging players to achieve the highest possible score by making rapid matches and creating special power-up gems (e.g., bomb gems for area clear, line-clearing gems) through 4- or 5-gem matches. Successfully completing a level by reaching a target score within the time limit awards "Magic Stars," which are spent in a shop to unlock 6 unique totems offering passive bonuses and 9 different active rewards like score multipliers. Difficulty scales across 5 treasure rooms as grid layouts become more intricate and score targets increase, with failure occurring if the time limit expires before the required score is met.
name: 蒙特祖玛3

concept: **Ordia:** Ordia is a one-finger platformer where players control a new life form by tapping and dragging anywhere on the screen to aim a trajectory and set launch power, then releasing to propel the creature. The creature automatically adheres to specific green surfaces upon contact, establishing a new launch point from which players can initiate the next jump. Each of the 30 levels, spread across 3 distinct worlds, presents a unique environmental puzzle filled with hazards like spikes, moving platforms, and enemy creatures, requiring precise timing and trajectory planning to navigate. Progression involves successfully guiding the creature to the exit portal of each level, unlocking subsequent stages and additional "challenge modes" or bonus levels for achieving secondary objectives like collecting all hidden orbs. Failure results from colliding with any hazard or falling into a void, necessitating a restart from the last checkpoint.
name: Ordia

concept: **MiniGames - 워치 게임 아케이드 (MiniGames - Watch Game Arcade):** MiniGames is a collection of 24 diverse arcade titles, with "Tube Twist" being a prominent endless runner where players guide a character automatically moving forward within a rotating tube. Players swipe left or right on the screen, or use the Apple Watch's Digital Crown, to rotate the tube and align the runner to avoid static obstacles like holes and spikes. Collecting yellow power orbs scattered within the tube charges a speed boost meter, which players can activate by tapping the runner, consuming the meter for a temporary speed increase and allowing them to hit red jump pads for bonus points. The game's difficulty scales as the runner's base speed gradually increases and obstacle patterns become more complex, with the primary objective being to achieve the highest possible distance score before colliding with any hazard.
name: MiniGames - 워치 게임 아케이드

concept: **Tilt to Live:** Tilt to Live is an arcade survival game where players control a small, central dot by tilting their iOS device to navigate it around the screen, avoiding an endless swarm of enemy dots that continuously spawn and home in on the player. Periodically, power-up orbs appear, which the player collects by touching them, granting temporary defensive buffs or activating one of 6 unlockable "Super-Weapons" like heat-seeking missiles or a flash-freezing blast. The game features 4 distinct gametypes, each with unique enemy behaviors and spawn rates that increase in density and speed over time, escalating difficulty. Progression involves completing 46 "Award Challenges" (e.g., survive for 60 seconds, destroy 100 enemies) to unlock new gametypes and the powerful Super-Weapons, with the primary objective being to achieve the highest possible score before colliding with an enemy dot.
name: Tilt to Live

concept: Carcassonne - Défi & Stratégie: This digital board game adaptation tasks players with drawing and placing square landscape tiles onto a shared grid, expanding a medieval map with cities, roads, abbeys, and fields. Players tap to select a tile from a communal draw pile, then drag and drop or tap to place it adjacent to existing tiles, with an option to rotate the tile by tapping before placement. After placing a tile, players can optionally place one of their limited Meeples onto a feature on that tile (e.g., a knight on a city segment, a thief on a road segment) to claim it, earning points when the feature is completed (e.g., a city is enclosed, a road connects two points). The game concludes when all 72 tiles have been placed, with final scoring for incomplete features and farmers in fields, and victory awarded to the player with the highest total points. Difficulty scales through four distinct AI behaviors, from casual to the challenging "Conqueror" AI, while six extensions like "Inns & Cathedrals" introduce new tile types, scoring rules, and strategic elements, some unlocked for free via an Asmodee account and others available for purchase.
name: Carcassonne - Défi & Stratégie
